widgets.name: AlphaFunction

widgets.name: Animatable
widgets.description: The class that represents an object capable of animation is the parent class of the minimum unit of visual, View.     When a property is an animatable property, it means that its value can change continuously as the target of an animation.     In this case, if a property notification callback is set, you can receive the callback according to the changing values.     Additionally, users can also add their own properties.

widgets.name: Animation
widgets.description:
Animation can be used to animate the properties of any number of objects, typically view.
If the "Finished" event is connected to a member function of an object, it must be disconnected before the object is destroyed.
This is typically done in the object destructor, and requires either the animation handle to be stored.
The overall animation time is superseded by the values given in the animation time used when calling the AnimateTo(), AnimateBy(), AnimateBetween() and AnimatePath() methods.
If any of the individual calls to those functions exceeds the overall animation time (Duration), then the overall animation time is automatically extended.

properties.name: Finished
properties.description:
Animation can be used to animate the properties of any number of objects, typically view.
If the "Finished" event is connected to a member function of an object, it must be disconnected before the object is destroyed.
This is typically done in the object destructor, and requires either the animation handle to be stored.
The overall animation time is superseded by the values given in the animation time used when calling the AnimateTo(), AnimateBy(), AnimateBetween() and AnimatePath() methods.
If any of the individual calls to those functions exceeds the overall animation time (Duration), then the overall animation time is automatically extended.

properties.name: ProgressReached
properties.description:
Event for the ProgressReached signal, which can be used to subscribe or unsubscribe the event handler.
The ProgressReached signal is emitted when the animation has reached a given progress percentage, this is set in the api SetProgressNotification.

properties.name: EndActions
properties.description: Enumeration for what to do when the animation ends, stopped, or destroyed.

properties.name: Interpolation
properties.description: When the animation ends, the animated property values are saved.

properties.name: States
properties.description: Values in between key frames are interpolated using a linear polynomial. (Default)

properties.name: LoopingModes
properties.description: The animation has stopped.

properties.name: Duration
properties.description: When the animation arrives at the end in looping mode, the animation restarts from the beginning. (Default)

properties.name: DefaultAlphaFunction
properties.description: Gets or sets the default alpha function for the animation.         This DefaultAlphaFunction is only applied to the animations are added after the DefaultAlphaFunction is set.

properties.name: State
properties.description: Queries the state of the animation.

properties.name: LoopCount
properties.description:
Set: Enables looping for a specified number of repeats. A zero is the same as Looping = true; i.e., repeat forever.
This property resets the looping value and should not be used with the Looping property.
Setting this parameter does not cause the animation to Play().
Get: Gets the loop count. A zero is the same as Looping = true; i.e., repeat forever.
The loop count is initially 1 for play once.

properties.name: Looping
properties.description:
Gets or sets the status of whether the animation will loop.
This property resets the loop count and should not be used with the LoopCount property.
Setting this parameter does not cause the animation to Play().

properties.name: EndAction
properties.description:
Gets or sets the end action of the animation.
This action is performed when the animation ends or if it is stopped.
The default end action is EndActions.Cancel.

properties.name: CurrentLoop
properties.description:
Gets the current loop count.
A value 0 indicating the current loop count when looping.

properties.name: DisconnectAction
properties.description:
Gets or sets the disconnect action.
If any of the animated property owners are disconnected from the stage while the animation is being played, then this action is performed.
The default action is EndActions.StopFinal.

properties.name: CurrentProgress
properties.description:
Gets or sets the progress of the animation.
The animation will play (or continue playing) from this point.
The progress must be in the 0-1 interval or in the play range interval if defined
otherwise, it will be ignored.

properties.name: SpeedFactor
properties.description:
Gets or sets specifications of a speed factor for the animation.
The speed factor is a multiplier of the normal velocity of the animation.
Values between [0, 1] will slow down the animation and values above one will speed up the animation.
It is also possible to specify a negative multiplier to play the animation in reverse.

properties.name: PlayRange
properties.description:
Gets or sets the playing range.
Animation will play between the values specified. Both values (range.x and range.y ) should be between 0-1, otherwise they will be ignored. If the range provided is not in proper order (minimum, maximum ), it will be reordered.

properties.name: ProgressNotification
properties.description:
Gets or sets the progress notification marker which triggers the ProgressReachedSignal.
Percentage of animation progress should be greater than 0 and less than 1, for example, 0.3 for 30%
One notification can be set on each animation.

properties.name: LoopingMode
properties.description: Enumeration for what looping mode is in.

properties.name: BlendPoint
properties.description: Sets and Gets the blend point to interpolate animate property         BlendPoint is a value between [0,1], If the value of the keyframe whose progress is 0 is different from the current value,         the property is animated as it smoothly blends until the progress reaches the blendPoint.

properties.name: ID
properties.description: Gets the animation's ID. 0 if animation is invalid.         Read-only

properties.name: PropertyList
properties.description: Gets or sets the properties of the animation.

properties.name: DestValueList
properties.description: Get the list of the destination value for each property of the animation.

properties.name: StartTimeList
properties.description: Get the list of the start time for each property of the animation.

properties.name: EndTimeList
properties.description: Get the list of end time for each property of the animation.

methods.name: AnimatePath
methods.signature: void AnimatePath(View view, Path path, Vector3 forward, AlphaFunction alphaFunction = null)
methods.description:
Animates the view's position and orientation through a predefined path.
The view will rotate to orient the supplied forward vector with the path's tangent.
If forward is the zero vector then no rotation will happen.

methods.name: AnimatePath
methods.signature: void AnimatePath(View view, Path path, Vector3 forward, int startTime, int endTime, AlphaFunction alphaFunction = null)
methods.description:
Animates the view's position and orientation through a predefined path.
The view will rotate to orient the supplied forward vector with the path's tangent.
If forward is the zero vector then no rotation will happen.

methods.name: Play
methods.description:
Creates an initialized animation.
The animation will not loop.
The default end action is "Cancel".
The default alpha function is linear.

methods.name: PlayFrom
methods.description:
Plays the animation from a given point.
The progress must be in the 0-1 interval or in the play range interval if defined, otherwise, it will be ignored.

methods.name: PlayAfter
methods.description:
Plays the animation after a given delay time.
The delay time is not included in the looping time.
When the delay time is a negative value, it would treat as play immediately.

methods.name: Pause
methods.description: Pauses the animation.

methods.name: Stop
methods.description: Stops the animation.

methods.name: Clear
methods.description:
Clears the animation.
This disconnects any objects that were being animated, effectively stopping the animation.

widgets.name: Path
widgets.description: A 3D parametric curve.     Paths can be used to animate the position and orientation of views.

properties.name: Points
properties.description: A 3D parametric curve.     Paths can be used to animate the position and orientation of views.

properties.name: ControlPoints
properties.description: Gets or sets the control points of the path.         The property value represents the array of control points defining the path.

methods.name: AddPoint
methods.description: A 3D parametric curve.     Paths can be used to animate the position and orientation of views.

methods.name: AddControlPoint
methods.description: Adds a control point to the path.

methods.name: GenerateControlPoints
methods.description:
Automatic generation of control points. Generated control points which result in a smooth join between the splines of each segment.
The generating algorithm is as follows:
For a given knot point K[N], find the vector that bisects K[N-1],[N] and [N],[N+1].
Calculate the tangent vector by taking the normal of this bisector.
The in control point is the length of the preceding segment back along this bisector multiplied by the curvature.
The out control point is the length of the succeeding segment forward along this bisector multiplied by the curvature.

methods.name: Sample
methods.signature: void Sample(float progress, Vector3 position, Vector3 tangent)
methods.description: Sample path at a given progress. Calculates the position and tangent at that point of the curve.

methods.description: An accessor for the control points.

methods.description: Gets the number of interpolation points in the path.

widgets.name: CustomView
widgets.description: CustomView provides some common functionality required by all views.

properties.description: CustomView provides some common functionality required by all views.

properties.description: Sets or gets whether this control is a focus group for keyboard navigation.

methods.name: SetBackground
methods.description: CustomView provides some common functionality required by all views.

methods.name: EnableGestureDetection
methods.description:
Allows deriving classes to enable any of the gesture detectors that are available.
Gesture detection can be enabled one at a time or in a bitwise format.

methods.name: OnInitialize
methods.description:
This method is called after the CustomView has been initialized.
After OnInitialize, the view will apply the style if it exists in the theme or it was given from constructor.
Derived classes should do any second phase initialization by overriding this method.

methods.name: OnStageConnection
methods.description:
Called after the view has been connected to the stage.
When a view is connected, it will be directly or indirectly parented to the root view.
The root view is provided automatically by Tizen.NUI.Stage, and is always considered to be connected.
When the parent of a set of views is connected to the stage, then all of the children will receive this callback.

methods.name: OnStageDisconnection
methods.description:
Called after the view has been disconnected from the stage.
If a view is disconnected, it either has no parent, or is parented to a disconnected view.
When the parent of a set of views is disconnected to the stage, then all of the children will receive this callback, starting with the leaf views.

methods.name: OnSceneConnection
methods.description:
Called after the view has been connected to the scene.
When a view is connected, it will be directly or indirectly parented to the root view.
The root view is provided automatically by Tizen.NUI.Window, and is always considered to be connected.
When the parent of a set of views is connected to the scene, then all of the children will receive this callback.

methods.name: OnSceneDisconnection
methods.description:
Called after the view has been disconnected from the scene.
If a view is disconnected, it either has no parent, or is parented to a disconnected view.
When the parent of a set of views is disconnected to the scene, then all of the children will receive this callback, starting with the leaf views.

methods.name: OnChildAdd
methods.description: Called after a child has been added to the owning view.

methods.name: OnChildRemove
methods.description: Called after the owning view has attempted to remove a child( regardless of whether it succeeded or not ).

methods.name: OnPropertySet
methods.description: Called when the owning view property is set.

methods.name: OnSizeSet
methods.description: Called when the owning view's size is set, for example, using View.SetSize().

methods.name: OnSizeAnimation
methods.description: Called when the owning view's size is animated, for example, using Animation::AnimateTo( Property ( view, View::Property::SIZE ), ... ).

methods.name: OnTouch
methods.description:
Called after a touch event is received by the owning view.
CustomViewBehaviour.REQUIRES_TOUCH_EVENTS must be enabled during construction. See CustomView(ViewWrapperImpl.CustomViewBehaviour behaviour).

methods.name: OnHover
methods.description:
Called after a hover event is received by the owning view.
CustomViewBehaviour.REQUIRES_HOVER_EVENTS must be enabled during construction. See CustomView(ViewWrapperImpl.CustomViewBehaviour behaviour).

methods.name: OnKey
methods.description: Called after a key event is received by the view that has had its focus set.

methods.name: OnWheel
methods.description:
Called after a wheel event is received by the owning view.
CustomViewBehaviour.REQUIRES_WHEEL_EVENTS must be enabled during construction. See CustomView(ViewWrapperImpl.CustomViewBehaviour behaviour).

methods.name: OnRelayout
methods.description:
Called after the size negotiation has been finished for this control.
The control is expected to assign this given size to itself or its children.
Should be overridden by derived classes if they need to layout views differently after certain operations like add or remove views, resize, or after changing specific properties.
As this function is called from inside the size negotiation algorithm, you cannot call RequestRelayout (the call would just be ignored).

methods.name: OnSetResizePolicy
methods.description: Notification for deriving classes.

methods.name: GetNaturalSize
methods.description: Returns the natural size of the view.

methods.name: CalculateChildSize
methods.description: Calculates the size for a child.

methods.name: GetHeightForWidth
methods.description:
This method is called during size negotiation when a height is required for a given width.
Derived classes should override this if they wish to customize the height returned.

methods.name: GetWidthForHeight
methods.description:
This method is called during size negotiation when a width is required for a given height.
Derived classes should override this if they wish to customize the width returned.

methods.name: RelayoutDependentOnChildren
methods.description: Determines if this view is dependent on it's children for relayout.

methods.name: RelayoutDependentOnChildren
methods.description: Determines if this view is dependent on it's children for relayout from the base class.

methods.name: OnCalculateRelayoutSize
methods.description: The virtual method to notify deriving classes that relayout dependencies have been         met and the size for this object is about to be calculated for the given dimension.

methods.name: OnLayoutNegotiated
methods.description: The virtual method to notify deriving classes that the size for a dimension has just been negotiated.

methods.name: OnStyleChange
methods.description: This method should be overridden by deriving classes requiring notifications when the style changes.

methods.name: OnFocusGained
methods.description: Called when the control gain key input focus. Should be overridden by derived classes if they need to customize what happens when the focus is gained.

methods.name: OnFocusLost
methods.description: Called when the control loses key input focus. Should be overridden by derived classes if they need to customize what happens when the focus is lost.

methods.name: GetNextFocusableView
methods.signature: View GetNextFocusableView(View currentFocusedView, View.FocusDirection direction, bool loopEnabled)
methods.description:
Gets the next keyboard focusable view in this control towards the given direction.
A control needs to override this function in order to support two dimensional keyboard navigation.

methods.name: OnFocusChangeCommitted
methods.description:
Informs this control that its chosen focusable view will be focused.
This allows the application to preform any actions it wishes before the focus is actually moved to the chosen view.

methods.name: OnKeyboardEnter
methods.description:
This method is called when the control has enter pressed on it.
Derived classes should override this to perform custom actions.

methods.name: OnPan
methods.description:
Called whenever a pan gesture is detected on this control.
This should be overridden by deriving classes when pan detection is enabled.
There is no default behavior with panning.
Pan detection should be enabled via EnableGestureDetection().

methods.name: OnTap
methods.description:
Called whenever a tap gesture is detected on this control.
This should be overridden by deriving classes when tap detection is enabled.
There is no default behavior with a tap.
Tap detection should be enabled via EnableGestureDetection().

widgets.name: FlexContainer
widgets.description:
FlexContainer implements a subset of the flexbox spec (defined by W3C):https://www.w3.org/TR/css3-flexbox/
It aims at providing a more efficient way to layout, align, and distribute space among items in the container, even when their size is unknown or dynamic.
FlexContainer has the ability to alter the width and the height of its children (i.e., flex items) to fill the available space in the best possible way on different screen sizes.
FlexContainer can expand items to fill available free space, or shrink them to prevent overflow.

properties.name: FlexDirectionType
properties.description:
FlexContainer implements a subset of the flexbox spec (defined by W3C):https://www.w3.org/TR/css3-flexbox/
It aims at providing a more efficient way to layout, align, and distribute space among items in the container, even when their size is unknown or dynamic.
FlexContainer has the ability to alter the width and the height of its children (i.e., flex items) to fill the available space in the best possible way on different screen sizes.
FlexContainer can expand items to fill available free space, or shrink them to prevent overflow.

properties.name: ContentDirectionType
properties.description: The flexible items are displayed vertically as a column.

properties.name: Justification
properties.description: Inherits the same direction from the parent.

properties.name: Alignment
properties.description: Items are positioned at the beginning of the container.

properties.name: WrapType
properties.description: Inherits the same alignment from the parent (only valid for "alignSelf" property).

properties.name: ContentDirection
properties.description: Flex items laid out in single line (shrunk to fit the flex container along the main axis).

properties.name: FlexDirection
properties.description: The direction of the main axis which determines the direction that flex items are laid out.

properties.name: FlexWrap
properties.description: Whether the flex items should wrap or not if there is no enough room for them on one flex line.

properties.name: JustifyContent
properties.description: The alignment of flex items when the items do not use all available space on the main axis.

properties.name: AlignItems
properties.description: The alignment of flex items when the items do not use all available space on the cross axis.

properties.name: AlignContent
properties.description: Similar to "alignItems", but it aligns flex lines; so only works when there are multiple lines.

properties.description:
ImageView PixelArea, type Vector4 (Animatable property).
Pixel area is a relative value with the whole image area as [0.0, 0.0, 1.0, 1.0].

properties.description:
The border of the image in the order: left, right, bottom, top.
If set, ImageMap will be ignored.
For N-Patch images only.
Optional.

properties.description:
Gets or sets whether to draw the borders only (if true).
If not specified, the default is false.
For N-Patch images only.
Optional.

properties.description: Gets or sets whether to synchronous loading the resourceurl of image.

properties.description: Gets or sets whether the image of the ResourceUrl property will be loaded synchronously.

properties.description: Gets or sets whether to automatically correct the orientation of an image.

properties.name: SynchronousSizing
properties.description:
Gets or sets whether to automatically reload the image as the view size
If we set this value as true, view size will be works as desired size of image.

properties.name: MaskingMode
properties.description: Gets or sets whether to apply mask on GPU or not.

properties.name: FastTrackUploading
properties.description: Gets or sets whether to apply fast track uploading or not.

properties.name: AlphaMaskURL
properties.description: Gets the loading state of the visual resource.

properties.name: CropToMask
properties.description: Whether to crop image to mask or scale mask to fit image.

properties.name: FittingMode
properties.description: Actions property value for Reload image.

properties.name: SamplingMode
properties.description:
Gets or sets filtering options used when resizing images to the sample original pixels.
If not supplied, the default is SamplingModeType.BoxThenLinear.
For normal quad images only.
Optional.

properties.name: DesiredWidth
properties.description: This method allows users to configure the blending of two images(previous and currnet) using alpha values.

properties.name: DesiredHeight
properties.description:
Gets or sets the desired image height.
If not specified, the actual image height is used.
For normal quad images only.
Optional.

properties.name: ReleasePolicy
properties.description:
Gets or sets ReleasePolicy for image.
If not supplied, the default is ReleasePolicyType.Detached.

properties.name: WrapModeU
properties.description:
Gets or sets the wrap mode for the u coordinate.
It decides how the texture should be sampled when the u coordinate exceeds the range of 0.0 to 1.0.
If not specified, the default is WrapModeType.Default(CLAMP).
For normal quad images only.
Optional.

properties.name: WrapModeV
properties.description:
Gets or sets the wrap mode for the v coordinate.
It decides how the texture should be sampled when the v coordinate exceeds the range of 0.0 to 1.0.
The first two elements indicate the top-left position of the area, and the last two elements are the areas of the width and the height respectively.
If not specified, the default is WrapModeType.Default(CLAMP).
For normal quad images only. Optional.

properties.description: Gets or sets the mode to adjust view size to preserve the aspect ratio of the image resource.

widgets.description: The base class of style attributes for a view.     This class provides a base for defining styles that can be applied to views.

properties.name: IncludeDefaultStyle
properties.description: The base class of style attributes for a view.     This class provides a base for defining styles that can be applied to views.

properties.name: BackgroundImage
properties.description: Gets or sets the image resource url of the background of view.         The mutually exclusive with "BackgroundColor". Setting it overwrites existing "BackgroundColor".

properties.name: Size2D
properties.description: Whether the children of this view can be focusable by keyboard navigation. If user sets this to false, the children of this view will not be focused.         Note : Default value is true.

properties.name: Position2D
properties.description: Defines view's opacity value.         This property allows you to specify different opacity values for various states of the view,         such as normal, pressed, focused, etc.

properties.name: ParentOrigin
properties.description: Determines whether the pivot point should be used to determine the position of the view.

properties.name: PivotPoint
properties.description:
Gets or sets the pivot point of a view.
This is expressed in unit coordinates, such that (0.0, 0.0, 0.5) is the top-left corner of the view, and (1.0, 1.0, 0.5) is the bottom-right corner.
The default pivot point is PivotPoint.Center (0.5, 0.5, 0.5).
A view position is the distance between its parent origin and this pivot point.
A view's orientation is the rotation from its default orientation, the rotation is centered around its pivot point.

properties.name: Position
properties.description: Gets or sets the width of the view.

properties.name: SizeModeFactor
properties.description:
Gets or sets the relative to parent size factor of the view.
This factor is only used when ResizePolicyType is set to either: ResizePolicyType.SizeRelativeToParent or ResizePolicyType.SizeFixedOffsetFromParent.
This view's size is set to the view's size multiplied by or added to this factor, depending on ResizePolicyType.

properties.name: Padding
properties.description: Gets or sets the width resize policy to be used.

properties.name: MinimumSize
properties.description: Gets or sets the minimum size the view can be assigned in size negotiation.

properties.name: MaximumSize
properties.description: Gets or sets the maximum size the view can be assigned in size negotiation.

properties.name: Size
properties.description: Sets the size of a view for the width, the height, and the depth.

properties.name: Margin
properties.description: Gets or sets the Margin for use in layout.

properties.name: BackgroundColor
properties.description: Gets or sets the color of the background of view.         The mutually exclusive with "BackgroundImage". Setting it overwrites existing "BackgroundImage".

properties.name: Color
properties.description: Color

properties.name: BackgroundImageBorder
properties.description: View BackgroundBorder

properties.name: ImageShadow
properties.description: Describes a shadow as an image for a View.         It is null by default.

properties.name: BoxShadow
properties.description: Describes a box shaped shadow drawing for a View.

properties.name: CornerRadius
properties.description: The radius for the rounded corners of the View.         The values in Vector4 are used in clockwise order from top-left to bottom-left : Vector4(top-left-corner, top-right-corner, bottom-right-corner, bottom-left-corner).         Each radius will clamp internally to the half of smaller of the view's width or height.

properties.name: BorderlineColor
properties.description: Whether the CornerRadius property value is relative (percentage [0.0f to 0.5f] of the view size) or absolute (in world units).         It is absolute by default.         When the policy is relative, the corner radius is relative to the smaller of the view's width and height.

properties.name: BorderlineColorSelector
properties.description: The color selector for the borderline of the View.

properties.name: SolidNull
properties.description: The Relative offset for the borderline of the View.         Recommended range : [-1.0f to 1.0f].         If -1.0f, draw borderline inside of the View.         If 1.0f, draw borderline outside of the View.         If 0.0f, draw borderline half inside and half outside.

widgets.name: TableView
widgets.description:
TableView is a layout container for aligning child actors in a grid like layout.
TableView constraints the X and the Y position and the width and the height of the child actors.
The Z position and depth are left intact so that the 3D model actors can also be laid out in a grid without loosing their depth scaling.

properties.name: LayoutPolicy
properties.description:
TableView is a layout container for aligning child actors in a grid like layout.
TableView constraints the X and the Y position and the width and the height of the child actors.
The Z position and depth are left intact so that the 3D model actors can also be laid out in a grid without loosing their depth scaling.

properties.description: Fixed with the given value.

properties.name: Columns

properties.name: LayoutRows
properties.description: This property allows setting the number of rows in the table view layout, which can affect how child views are arranged within the table.

properties.name: LayoutColumns
properties.description: Gets or sets the number of layout columns.

properties.name: rowIndex
properties.description:
Adds a child to the table.
If the row or column index is outside the table, the table gets resized bigger.

properties.description: Gets or sets the index of a column.

TableView constraints the X and the Y position and the width and the height of the child actors.
The Z position and depth are left intact so that the 3D model actors can also be laid out in a grid without loosing their depth scaling.

Removed elements are deleted.

Removed elements are deleted.

methods.name: GetCellPadding
methods.description: Gets the current padding as width and height.

methods.name: SetFitHeight
methods.description: Specifies this row as fitting its height to its children.

methods.name: IsFitHeight
methods.description: Checks if the row is a fit row.

methods.name: SetFitWidth
methods.description: Specifies this column as fitting its width to its children.

methods.name: IsFitWidth
methods.description: Checks if the column is a fit column.

methods.name: SetFixedHeight
methods.description:
Sets a row to have a fixed height.
Setting a fixed height of 0 has no effect.

methods.name: GetFixedHeight
methods.description: Gets a row's fixed height.

methods.name: SetRelativeHeight
methods.description:
Sets a row to have a relative height. Relative height means percentage of the remainder of the table height after subtracting padding and fixed height rows.
Setting a relative height of 0 has no effect.

methods.name: GetRelativeHeight
methods.description: Gets a row's relative height.

methods.name: SetFixedWidth
methods.description:
Sets a column to have a fixed width.
Setting a fixed width of 0 has no effect.

methods.name: GetFixedWidth
methods.description: Gets a column's fixed width.

methods.name: SetRelativeWidth
methods.description:
Sets a column to have a relative width. Relative width means percentage of the remainder of the table width after subtracting padding and fixed width columns.
Setting a relative width of 0 has no effect.

methods.name: GetRelativeWidth
methods.description: Gets a column's relative width.

methods.name: SetCellAlignment
methods.signature: void SetCellAlignment(TableView.CellPosition position, HorizontalAlignmentType horizontal, VerticalAlignmentType vertical)
methods.description:
Sets the alignment on a cell.
Cells without calling this function have the default values of left and top respectively.

properties.name: EnableGrabHandle
properties.description: Scroll the text control by specific amount..

properties.name: EnableGrabHandlePopup
properties.description:
The Enable grab handle popup property.
Enables the grab handle popup for text selection.
The default value is true, which means the grab handle popup is enabled by default.

properties.name: MinLineSize
properties.description:
Minimum line size to be used.
The height of the line in points.
If the font size is larger than the line size, it works with the font size.

properties.name: CharacterSpacing
properties.description:
The spaces between characters in Pixels. A positive value will make the characters far apart (expanded) and a negative value will bring them closer (condensed).
The default value is 0.f which does nothing.

properties.name: RemoveFrontInset
properties.description:
The RemoveFrontInset property.
This property is used when the xBearing of first glyph must not be trimmed.
When set to false, The gap between (0, 0) from the first glyph's leftmost pixel is included in the width of text label.

properties.name: RemoveBackInset
properties.description:
The RemoveBackInset property.
This property is used when the advance of last glyph must not be trimmed.
When set to false, The gap between the last glyph's rightmost pixel and X coordinate that next glyph will be placed is included in the width of text label.

Sets the speed of scrolling in pixels per second.

properties.name: AutoScrollLoopCount
properties.description:
The AutoScrollLoopCount property.
Number of complete loops when scrolling enabled.

properties.name: AutoScrollGap
properties.description:
The AutoScrollGap property.
Gap before scrolling wraps.

properties.name: LineSpacing
properties.description:
The LineSpacing property.
The default extra space between lines in points.

properties.name: RelativeLineHeight
properties.description:
The relative height of the line (a factor that will be multiplied by text height).
If the value is less than 1, the lines could to be overlapped.

properties.name: Underline
properties.description:
The Underline property.
The default underline parameters.
The underline map contains the following keys :
enable (bool)Whether the underline is enabled (the default value is false) color (Color)The color of the underline (If not provided then the color of the text is used) height (float)The height in pixels of the underline (the default value is 1.f)

properties.name: Shadow
properties.description: Set Underline to TextLabel.

properties.name: TextShadow
properties.description: Set Shadow to TextLabel.

properties.name: Emboss
properties.description:
The Emboss property.
The default emboss parameters.

properties.name: Outline
properties.description:
Set Emboss to TextLabel.
The text emboss enable (bool)Whether the emboss is enabled (the default value is false) direction (Vector2)The emboss direction in texture space. (the default value is (0.0f, 0.0f).) strength (float)The strength of emboss in pixels. (the default value is 0.0f.) lightColor (Color)The highlight color for raised areas. (the default value is transparent.) shadowColor (Color)The shadow color for recessed areas. (the default value is transparent.)

properties.name: PixelSize
properties.description: Set Outline to TextLabel.

properties.name: Ellipsis
properties.description:
The Ellipsis property.
Enable or disable the ellipsis.

properties.name: EllipsisPosition
properties.description: The ellipsis position of the text. Specifies which portion of the text should be replaced with an ellipsis when the text size exceeds the layout size.

properties.name: EllipsisMode
properties.description: The EllipsisMode property.

properties.name: IsScrolling
properties.description: Whether the auto scroll animation is playing or not.

properties.name: RenderScale
properties.description: Renders a texture at a specified scale to prevent text rendering quality degradation when scaling up with View.Scale.

properties.name: PixelSnapFactor
properties.description: A factor of pixel snap.

properties.name: AutoScrollLoopDelay
properties.description:
The AutoScrollLoopDelay property.
The amount of time to delay the starting time of auto scrolling and further loops.

properties.name: AutoScrollStopMode
properties.description:
The AutoScrollStopMode property.
The auto scrolling stop behaviour.
The default value is AutoScrollStopMode.FinishLoop.

properties.name: LineCount
properties.description: Gets the line count of the text.

properties.name: LineWrapMode
properties.description:
The LineWrapMode property.
line wrap mode when the text lines over layout width.

properties.name: TextDirection
properties.description: The direction of the text such as left to right or right to left.

properties.name: VerticalLineAlignment
properties.description: The vertical line alignment of the text.

properties.name: MatchSystemLanguageDirection
properties.description: The text alignment to match the direction of the system language.

properties.name: TextFit
properties.description:
The text fit parameters.
The textFit map contains the following keys :
enable (bool)True to enable the text fit or false to disable (the default value is false) minSize (float)Minimum Size for text fit (the default value is 10.f) maxSize (float)Maximum Size for text fit (the default value is 100.f) stepSize (float)Step Size for font increase (the default value is 1.f) fontSize (string)The size type of font, You can choose between "pointSize" or "pixelSize". (the default value is "pointSize")

properties.name: MinLineSize
properties.description: Set TextFit to TextLabel.

properties.name: CharacterSpacing
properties.description:
The spaces between characters in Pixels. A positive value will make the characters far apart (expanded) and a negative value will bring them closer (condensed).
The default value is 0.f which does nothing.

properties.name: AnchorColor
properties.description:
The AnchorColor property.
The color of the anchor.
This property is used as the default color of the markup anchor tag.
If there is a color attribute in the markup anchor tag, the markup attribute takes precedence.

properties.name: AnchorClickedColor
properties.description:
The AnchorClickedColor property.
The color of the clicked anchor.
This property is used as the default clicked color of the markup anchor tag.
If there is a color attribute in the markup anchor tag, the markup attribute takes precedence.

properties.name: RemoveFrontInset
properties.description:
The RemoveFrontInset property.
This property is used when the xBearing of first glyph must not be trimmed.
When set to false, The gap between (0, 0) from the first glyph's leftmost pixel is included in the width of text label.

properties.name: RemoveBackInset
properties.description:
The RemoveBackInset property.
This property is used when the advance of last glyph must not be trimmed.
When set to false, The gap between the last glyph's rightmost pixel and X coordinate that next glyph will be placed is included in the width of text label.

properties.name: FontSizeScale
properties.description:
The FontSizeScale property for scaling the specified font size up or down.
The default value is 1.0.
The given font size scale value is used for multiplying the specified font size before querying fonts.
If FontSizeScale.UseSystemSetting, will use the SystemSettings.FontSize internally.

properties.name: EnableFontSizeScale
properties.description:
The EnableFontSizeScale property.
Whether the font size scale is enabled. (The default value is true)

properties.name: Cutout
properties.description: The cutout property.

properties.name: RenderMode
properties.description: The RenderMode property.

properties.name: ManualRendered
properties.description:
Whether the last async rendering result is a manual render.
If it's false, the render result was automatically requested by OnRelayout.

properties.name: AsyncLineCount
properties.description: Whether a render request is required when render mode is AsyncManual.

properties.name: LayoutDirectionPolicy
properties.description: The LayoutDirectionPolicy property.

properties.name: AutoScrollDirection
properties.description: The AutoScrollDirection.

properties.description: The font's family.

properties.description: The font's weight: one of {"thin", "ultraLight", "extraLight", "light", "demiLight", "semiLight", "book", "normal", "regular", "medium", "demiBold", "semiBold", "bold", "ultraBold", "extraBold", "black", "heavy", "extraBlack"}.

properties.name: FontWidth
properties.description: The font's width: one of {"ultraCondensed", "extraCondensed", "condensed", "semiCondensed", "normal", "semiExpanded", "expanded", "extraExpanded", "ultraExpanded"}.

properties.description: The type of layout: one of TextLayout {"singleLine", "multiLine", "circular"}

properties.description: The text alignment within the arc: one of CircularAlignment. The @p horizontalAlignment and @p verticalAlignment can be used to align the text within the text area.

properties.description: The radius in pixels of the circular text.

properties.name: BeginAngle
properties.description: The begin angle in degrees of the text area on the circle. The top of the circle is 0°, the right side 90°, the bottom 180° and the left 270°.

properties.name: IncrementAngle
properties.description: The increment angle in degrees of the text area on the circle. The @p incrementAngle defines a direction. If positive, the text will be laid out clockwise.

properties.name: EllipsisEnabled
properties.description: Whether the ellipsis layout option is enabled.

properties.name: RotatedSize
properties.description: The rotated size within the buffer of the embedded item.

properties.name: Angle
properties.description: Rotation angle of the pixel buffer in degrees.

properties.name: Input
properties.description: Whether the color of the image is multiplied by the color of the text.

properties.description: The color of the text.

methods.name: Render
methods.signature: PixelBuffer Render(RendererParameters textParameters, ref EmbeddedItemInfo[] embeddedItemLayout)
methods.description: Class with the text and style parameters to be rendered into a pixel buffer.

methods.name: CreateShadow
methods.description: Creates a shadow for the text given in the input pixel buffer.         The function returns a RGBA8888 pixel buffer with the text and its shadow rendered on it.         The pixel format of the @e input pixel buffer could be an A8 or an RGBA8888. If it's         an A8 pixel buffer, it uses the given @e textColor to give color to the text. Otherwise         it uses the color of the @e input pixel buffer.

methods.name: ConvertToRgba8888
methods.signature: PixelBuffer ConvertToRgba8888(PixelBuffer pixelBuffer, Vector4 color, bool multiplyByAlpha)
methods.description: Converts a @p pixelBuffer with pixel format A8 to RGBA8888 using the given @p color.         @note Does nothing if the @p pixelBuffer is not A8.

methods.name: UpdateBuffer
methods.signature: void UpdateBuffer(PixelBuffer src, PixelBuffer dst, uint x, uint y, bool blend)
methods.description: Updates the @p dst pixel buffer with the data from @p src pixel buffer.         @note Both pixel buffers must have the same pixel format. Does nothing if both pixel format are different.         @note The function does nothing if the @p src pixel buffer doesn't fit into the @p dst pixel buffer.         The @p src pixel buffer could be blended with the @p dst pixel buffer if @p blend is set to @e true.

methods.name: GetFontInfoList
methods.description: Splits the text in pages of the size given in @p textParameters         @note The returned indices are indices to utf32 characters. The input text is encoded in utf8.         <returns> An array with the indices of the last character of each page </returns>

methods.name: GetMapToTextFitArray
methods.description: This method converts a TextFitArray property map to a TextFitArray and returns it.         <param name="textFitArrayMap">The TextFitArray PropertyMap.</param>         <returns> A TextFitArray struct. </returns>

methods.name: GetFontSizeScale
methods.description: It returns a float value according to SystemSettingsFontSize.         The returned value can be used for FontSizeScale property.         <param name="systemSettingsFontSize">The SystemSettingsFontSize enum value.</param>         <returns> A float value for FontSizeScale property. </returns>

methods.name: AddRect
methods.signature: bool AddRect(float x, float y, float width, float height, float roundedCornerX, float roundedCornerY)
methods.description: Shape is a command list for drawing one shape groups It has own path data and properties for sync/asynchronous drawing

methods.name: AddCircle
methods.signature: bool AddCircle(float x, float y, float radiusX, float radiusY)
methods.description: Append a circle with given center and x,y-axis radius

methods.name: AddArc
methods.signature: bool AddArc(float x, float y, float radius, float startAngle, float sweep, bool pie)
methods.description: Append the arcs.

methods.name: AddMoveTo
methods.description: Add a point that sets the given point as the current point,         implicitly starting a new subpath and closing the previous one.

methods.name: AddLineTo
methods.description: Adds a straight line from the current position to the given end point.         After the line is drawn, the current position is updated to be at the         end point of the line.         If no current position present, it draws a line to itself, basically * a point.

methods.name: AddCubicTo
methods.signature: bool AddCubicTo(float controlPoint1X, float controlPoint1Y, float controlPoint2X, float controlPoint2Y, float endPointX, float endPointY)
methods.description: Adds a cubic Bezier curve between the current position and the         given end point (lineEndPoint) using the control points specified by         (controlPoint1), and (controlPoint2). After the path is drawn,         the current position is updated to be at the end point of the path.

methods.name: AddPath
methods.description: Appends a given sub-path to the path.         The current point value is set to the last point from the sub-path.         @note The interface is designed for optimal path setting if the caller has a completed path commands already.

methods.name: Close
methods.description: Closes the current subpath by drawing a line to the beginning of the         subpath, automatically starting a new path. The current point of the         new path is (0, 0).         If the subpath does not contain any points, this function does nothing.

methods.name: ResetPath
methods.description: Reset the added path(rect, circle, path, etc...) information.         Color and Stroke information are keeped.

methods.description: VideoView is a control for video playback and display.

methods.name: EnableOffscreenFrameRendering
methods.description: Enables or disables offscreen frame rendering for video interpolation.         When enabled, the video player will use offscreen rendering for frame interpolation,         which can improve visual quality for certain video content.

methods.name: Play
methods.description: Starts the video playback.

methods.name: Pause
methods.description: Pauses the video playback.

methods.name: Stop
methods.description: Stops the video playback.

methods.name: Forward
methods.description: Seeks forward by the specified number of milliseconds.

methods.name: Backward
methods.description: Seeks backward by the specified number of milliseconds.

widgets.name: View
widgets.description: View is the base class for all views.

properties.name: ViewAccessibilityMode
properties.description: View is the base class for all views.

properties.name: ViewResizePolicyMode
properties.description: Default accessibility implementation. Overriding View.Accessibility...()             virtual methods will have no effect.

properties.name: LayoutingDisabled
properties.description: Default implementation. Will consider ResizePolicy and Relayout implementations

properties.name: ControlState
properties.description: If set to true, the <see cref="GrabTouchAfterLeave"/> property value is set to true when all Views are created.

properties.name: ExcludeLayouting
properties.description: Gets / Sets the status of whether the view is excluded from its parent's layouting or not.

properties.name: StyleName
properties.description: The StyleName, type string.         The value indicates DALi style name defined in json theme file.

properties.name: KeyInputFocus
properties.description: The KeyInputFocus, type bool.

properties.name: BackgroundColor
properties.description: The mutually exclusive with "backgroundImage" and "background" type Vector4.

properties.name: BackgroundImage
properties.description: The mutually exclusive with "backgroundColor" and "background" type Map.

properties.name: BackgroundImageBorder
properties.description: Get or set the border of background image.

properties.name: ImageShadow
properties.description: Gets or sets the background of the view.         This property value is the PropertyMap representing the background.

properties.name: BoxShadow
properties.description: Describes a box shaped shadow drawing for a View.         It is null by default.

properties.name: InnerShadow
properties.description: Describes a inner shadow shadow drawing for a View.         It is null by default.

properties.name: CornerRadius
properties.description: The radius for the rounded corners of the View.         This will rounds background and shadow edges.         The values in Vector4 are used in clockwise order from top-left to bottom-left : Vector4(top-left-corner, top-right-corner, bottom-right-corner, bottom-left-corner).         Each radius will clamp internally to the half of smaller of the view's width or height.         Note that, an image background (or shadow) may not have rounded corners if it uses a Border property.

properties.name: CornerRadiusPolicy
properties.description: Whether the CornerRadius property value is relative (percentage [0.0f to 0.5f] of the view size) or absolute (in world units).         It is absolute by default.         When the policy is relative, the corner radius is relative to the smaller of the view's width and height.

properties.name: CornerSquareness
properties.description: The squareness for the rounded corners of the View.         This will make squircle background and shadow edges.         The values in Vector4 are used in clockwise order from top-left to bottom-left : Vector4(top-left-corner, top-right-corner, bottom-right-corner, bottom-left-corner).         Each squareness will clamp internally to the [0.0f to 1.0f].         If 0.0f, rounded corner applied.         If 1.0f, View will be rendered like a square.

properties.name: BorderlineWidth
properties.description: The width for the borderline of the View.

properties.name: BorderlineColor
properties.description: The color for the borderline of the View.         It is Color.Black by default.

properties.name: BorderlineOffset
properties.description: The color selector for the borderline of the View.         Like BackgroundColor, color selector typed BorderlineColor should be used in ViewStyle only.         So this API is internally used only.

properties.name: State
properties.description: Gets or sets the current state of the view.

properties.name: SubState
properties.description: The current sub state of the view.

properties.name: TooltipText
properties.description: Displays a tooltip         This property allows setting the tooltip properties such as text.

properties.name: Flex
properties.description:
The Child property of FlexContainer.
The proportion of the free space in the container, the flex item will receive.
If all items in the container set this property, their sizes will be proportional to the specified flex factor.

properties.name: AlignSelf
properties.description:
The Child property of FlexContainer.
The alignment of the flex item along the cross axis, which, if set, overrides the default alignment for all items in the container.

properties.name: FlexMargin
properties.description:
The Child property of FlexContainer.
The space around the flex item.

properties.name: CellIndex
properties.description: The top-left cell this child occupies, if not set, the first available cell is used.

properties.name: RowSpan
properties.description: The number of rows this child occupies, if not set, the default value is 1.

properties.name: ColumnSpan
properties.description: The number of columns this child occupies, if not set, the default value is 1.

properties.name: LeftFocusableView
properties.description: The horizontal alignment of this child inside the cells, if not set, the default value is 'left'.

properties.name: RightFocusableView
properties.description:
The right focusable view.
This will return null if not set.
This will also return null if the specified right focusable view is not on a window.

properties.name: UpFocusableView
properties.description:
The up focusable view.
This will return null if not set.
This will also return null if the specified up focusable view is not on a window.

properties.name: DownFocusableView
properties.description:
The down focusable view.
This will return null if not set.
This will also return null if the specified down focusable view is not on a window.

properties.name: ClockwiseFocusableView
properties.description:
The clockwise focusable view by rotary wheel.
This will return null if not set.
This will also return null if the specified clockwise focusable view is not on a window.

properties.name: CounterClockwiseFocusableView
properties.description:
The counter clockwise focusable view by rotary wheel.
This will return null if not set.
This will also return null if the specified counter clockwise focusable view is not on a window.

properties.name: Focusable
properties.description: Whether the view should be focusable by keyboard navigation.

properties.name: FocusableChildren
properties.description: Whether the children of this view can be focusable by keyboard navigation. If user sets this to false, the children of this actor view will not be focused.         Note : Default value is true.

properties.name: FocusableInTouch
properties.description: Whether this view can focus by touch.         If Focusable is false, FocusableInTouch is disabled.         If you want to have focus on touch, you need to set both Focusable and FocusableInTouch settings to true.

properties.name: CurrentPosition
properties.description: Retrieves the position of the view.         The coordinates are relative to the view's parent.

properties.name: Size2D
properties.description:
Sets the size of a view for the width and the height.
Geometry can be scaled to fit within this area.
This does not interfere with the view's scale factor.
The views default depth is the minimum of width and height.

properties.name: CurrentSize
properties.description: Retrieves the size of the view.         The coordinates are relative to the view's parent.

properties.name: Opacity
properties.description: Retrieves and sets the view's opacity.

properties.name: Position2D
properties.description:
Sets the position of the view for X and Y.
By default, sets the position vector between the parent origin and the pivot point (default).
If the position inheritance is disabled, sets the world position.

properties.name: ScreenPosition
properties.description: Retrieves the screen position of the view.

properties.name: ScreenPositionSize
properties.description: Retrieves the screen position and size of the view.

properties.name: PositionUsesPivotPoint
properties.description: Determines whether the pivot point should be used to determine the position of the view.         This is false by default.

properties.name: PositionUsesAnchorPoint
properties.description: This has been deprecated in API5 and Will be removed in API8. Use PositionUsesPivotPoint instead.

properties.name: IsOnWindow
properties.description:
Queries whether the view is connected to the stage.
When a view is connected, it will be directly or indirectly parented to the root view.

properties.name: HierarchyDepth
properties.description: Gets the depth in the hierarchy for the view.

properties.name: SiblingOrder
properties.description: Sets the sibling order of the view so the depth position can be defined within the same parent.

properties.name: NaturalSize
properties.description: Returns the natural size of the view.

properties.name: NaturalSize2D
properties.description: Returns the natural size (Size2D) of the view.

properties.name: ParentOrigin
properties.description:
Gets or sets the origin of a view within its parent's area.
This is expressed in unit coordinates, such that (0.0, 0.0, 0.5) is the top-left corner of the parent, and (1.0, 1.0, 0.5) is the bottom-right corner.
The default parent-origin is ParentOrigin.TopLeft (0.0, 0.0, 0.5).
A view's position is the distance between this origin and the view's anchor-point.

properties.name: PivotPoint
properties.description:
Gets or sets the anchor-point of a view.
This is expressed in unit coordinates, such that (0.0, 0.0, 0.5) is the top-left corner of the view, and (1.0, 1.0, 0.5) is the bottom-right corner.
The default pivot point is PivotPoint.Center (0.5, 0.5, 0.5).
A view position is the distance between its parent-origin and this anchor-point.
A view's orientation is the rotation from its default orientation, the rotation is centered around its anchor-point.
The view has been initialized.

properties.name: SizeWidth
properties.description: Gets or sets the size width of the view.

properties.name: SizeHeight
properties.description: Gets or sets the size height of the view.

properties.name: Position
properties.description:
Gets or sets the position of the view.
By default, sets the position vector between the parent origin and pivot point (default).
If the position inheritance is disabled, sets the world position.

properties.name: PositionX
properties.description: Gets or sets the position X of the view.

properties.name: PositionY
properties.description: Gets or sets the position Y of the view.

properties.name: PositionZ
properties.description: Gets or sets the position Z of the view.

properties.name: WorldPosition
properties.description: Gets or sets the world position of the view.

properties.name: Orientation
properties.description:
Gets or sets the orientation of the view.
The view's orientation is the rotation from its default orientation, and the rotation is centered around its anchor-point.

properties.name: WorldOrientation
properties.description: Gets or sets the world orientation of the view.

properties.name: Scale
properties.description: Gets or sets the scale factor applied to the view.

properties.name: ScaleX
properties.description: Gets or sets the scale X factor applied to the view.

properties.name: ScaleY
properties.description: Gets or sets the scale Y factor applied to the view.

properties.name: ScaleZ
properties.description: Gets or sets the scale Z factor applied to the view.

properties.name: WorldScale
properties.description: Gets the world scale of the view.

properties.name: Visibility
properties.description: Retrieves the visibility flag of the view.

properties.name: WorldColor
properties.description: Gets the view's world color.

properties.name: Name
properties.description: Gets or sets the view's name.

properties.name: ChildCount
properties.description: Get the number of children held by the view.

properties.name: ID
properties.description: Gets The unique identifier of the view. (Read-only)

properties.name: Sensitive
properties.description: Gets or sets the status of whether the view should emit touch or hover signals.         If a View is made insensitive, then the View and its children are not hittable.

properties.name: IsEnabled
properties.description: Gets or sets the status of whether the view should be enabled user interactions.         If a View is made disabled, then user interactions including touch, focus, and actiavation is disabled.

properties.name: LeaveRequired
properties.description: Gets or sets the status of whether the view should receive a notification when touch or hover motion events leave the boundary of the view.

properties.name: InheritOrientation
properties.description: Gets or sets the status of whether a child view inherits it's parent's orientation.

properties.name: InheritScale
properties.description: Gets or sets the status of whether a child view inherits it's parent's scale.

properties.name: DrawMode
properties.description:
Gets or sets the status of how the view and its children should be drawn.
Not all views are renderable, but DrawMode can be inherited from any view.
If an object is in a 3D layer, it will be depth-tested against other objects in the world, i.e., it may be obscured if other objects are in front.
If DrawMode.Overlay2D is used, the view and its children will be drawn as a 2D overlay.
Overlay views are drawn in a separate pass, after all non-overlay views within the layer.
For overlay views, the drawing order is with respect to tree levels of views, and depth-testing will not be used.

properties.name: SizeModeFactor
properties.description:
Gets or sets the relative to parent size factor of the view.
This factor is only used when ResizePolicyType is set to either: ResizePolicyType.SizeRelativeToParent or ResizePolicyType.SizeFixedOffsetFromParent.
This view's size is set to the view's size multiplied by or added to this factor, depending on ResizePolicyType.

properties.name: WidthResizePolicy
properties.description: Gets or sets the width resize policy to be used.

properties.name: HeightResizePolicy
properties.description: Gets or sets the height resize policy to be used.

properties.name: SizeScalePolicy
properties.description:
Gets or sets the policy to use when setting size with size negotiation.
Defaults to SizeScalePolicyType.UseSizeSet.

properties.name: WidthForHeight
properties.description: Gets or sets the status of whether the width size is dependent on the height size.

properties.name: HeightForWidth
properties.description: Gets or sets the status of whether the height size is dependent on the width size.

properties.name: Padding
properties.description: Gets or sets the padding for use in layout.

properties.name: MinimumSize
properties.description: Gets or sets the minimum size the view can be assigned in size negotiation.

properties.name: MaximumSize
properties.description: Gets or sets the maximum size the view can be assigned in size negotiation.

properties.name: InheritPosition
properties.description:
Gets or sets whether a child view inherits it's parent's position.
Default is to inherit.
Switching this off means that using position sets the view's world position, i.e., translates from the world origin (0,0,0) to the pivot point of the view.

properties.name: ClippingMode
properties.description: Gets or sets the clipping behavior (mode) of it's children.

properties.name: RenderableCount
properties.description: Gets the number of user-defined renderables held by the view.

properties.name: EffectiveRenderableCount
properties.description: Gets the number of effective renderables, including both user-defined and system-generated.

properties.name: AnchorPoint
properties.description: This has been deprecated in API5 and will be removed in API8. Use PivotPoint instead.

properties.name: Size
properties.description:
Sets the size of a view for the width, the height and the depth.
Geometry can be scaled to fit within this area.
This does not interfere with the view's scale factor.
The views default depth is the minimum of width and height.

properties.name: Parent
properties.description: This has been deprecated in API5 and will be removed in API8. Use 'Container GetParent() for derived class' instead.

properties.name: InheritLayoutDirection
properties.description: Gets/Sets whether inherit parent's the layout Direction.

properties.name: LayoutDirection
properties.description: Gets/Sets the layout Direction.

properties.name: Margin
properties.description: Gets or sets the Margin for use in layout.

properties.name: WidthSpecification
properties.description: The required policy for this dimension, <see cref="LayoutParamPolicies"/> values or exact value.

properties.name: HeightSpecification
properties.description: The required policy for this dimension, <see cref="LayoutParamPolicies"/> values or exact value.

properties.name: LayoutTransitions
properties.description: Gets the List of transitions for this View.

properties.name: LayoutTransition
properties.description: Sets a layout transitions for this View.

properties.name: PaddingEX
properties.description: This has been deprecated in API5 and will be removed in API8. Use Padding instead.

properties.name: Color
properties.description: The Color of View. This is an RGBA value.         Each RGBA components match as <see cref="ColorRed"/>, <see cref="ColorGreen"/>, <see cref="ColorBlue"/>, and <see cref="Opacity"/>.         This property will multiply the final color of this view. (BackgroundColor, BorderlineColor, BackgroundImage, etc).         For example, if view.BackgroundColor = Color.Yellow and view.Color = Color.Purple, this view will shown as Red.         Inherient of color value depend on <see cref="ColorMode"/>.

properties.name: ColorRed
properties.description: The Red component of View.Color.

properties.name: ColorGreen
properties.description: The Green component of View.Color.

properties.name: ColorBlue
properties.description: The Blue component of View.Color.

properties.name: Layout
properties.description: Set the layout on this View. Replaces any existing Layout.

properties.name: Weight
properties.description: The weight of the View, used to share available space in a layout with siblings.

properties.name: BackgroundImageSynchronosLoading
properties.description: Whether to load the BackgroundImage synchronously.         If not specified, the default is false, i.e. the BackgroundImage is loaded asynchronously.         Note: For Normal Quad images only.

properties.name: BackgroundImageSynchronousLoading
properties.description: Whether to load the BackgroundImage synchronously.         If not specified, the default is false, i.e. the BackgroundImage is loaded asynchronously.         Note: For Normal Quad images only.

properties.name: UpdateAreaHint
properties.description: Gets and Sets hint of partial update area.

properties.name: EnableControlStatePropagation
properties.description: Enable/Disable ControlState propagation for children.         It is false by default.         If the View needs to share ControlState with descendants, please set it true.         Please note that, changing the value will also changes children's EnableControlStatePropagation value recursively.

properties.name: PropagatableControlStates
properties.description: The ControlStates can propagate from the parent.         Listed ControlStates will be accepted propagation of the parent ControlState changes         if parent view EnableControlState is true.         <see cref="EnableControlState"/>.         Default is ControlState.All, so every ControlStates will be propagated from the parent.

properties.name: EnableControlState
properties.description: By default, it is false in View, true in Control.         Note that if the value is true, the View will be a touch receptor.

properties.name: GrabTouchAfterLeave
properties.description: Whether the actor grab all touches even if touch leaves its boundary.

properties.name: AllowOnlyOwnTouch
properties.description: Whether the view will only receive own touch.

properties.name: BlendEquation
properties.description: Determines which blend equation will be used to render renderers of this actor.

properties.name: ThemeChangeSensitive
properties.description: If the value is true, the View will change its style as the theme changes.         The default value is false in normal case but it can be true when the NUIApplication is created with <see cref="NUIApplication.ThemeOptions.ThemeChangeSensitive"/>.

properties.name: Culled
properties.description: Create Style, it is abstract function and must be override.

properties.name: TransitionOptions
properties.description: Set or Get TransitionOptions for the page transition.         This property is used to define how this view will be transitioned during Page switching.

properties.name: VoiceInteractionName
properties.description: Called when the view is hit through TouchEvent or GestureEvent.         If it returns true, it means that it was hit, and the touch/gesture event is called from the view.         If it returns false, it means that it will not be hit, and the hit-test continues to the next view.         User can override whether hit or not in HitTest.         You can get the coordinates relative to tthe top-left of the hit view by touch.GetLocalPosition(0).         or you can get the coordinates relative to the top-left of the screen by touch.GetScreenPosition(0).

properties.name: OffScreenRendering
properties.description: Gets of sets the current offscreen rendering type of the view.

properties.name: Ignored
properties.description: Gets of sets the flag to identify the View will be ignored or not.         If the View is marked as ignored, it will not be rendered and will be excluded from render thread computation.         So, the render thread properties like WorldPosition and WorldColor become inaccurate.

properties.name: CurrentIgnored
properties.description: Gets the flag to identify the View is ignored or not currently.

properties.name: WorldIgnored
properties.description: Gets the flag to identify the View is world ignored or not currently.

properties.name: ChildrenDepthIndexPolicy
properties.description: The policy of children depth index generate.

events.name: ControlStateChangedEvent
events.description: View is the base class for all views.

methods.description: View is the base class for all views.

methods.name: SetDefaultAllowOnlyOwnTouch
methods.description: If set to true, the <see cref="AllowOnlyOwnTouch"/> property value is set to true when all Views are created.

methods.name: ApplyStyle
methods.description: Deprecate. Do not use this.         The style instance applied to this view.         Note that do not modify the ViewStyle.         Modifying ViewStyle will affect other views with same ViewStyle.

properties.description: Event when a child is removed.

properties.name: FocusLost
properties.description:
An event for the KeyInputFocusLost signal which can be used to subscribe or unsubscribe the event handler provided by the user.
The KeyInputFocusLost signal is emitted when the control loses the key input focus.

properties.name: KeyEvent
properties.description:
An event for the KeyPressed signal which can be used to subscribe or unsubscribe the event handler provided by the user.
The KeyPressed signal is emitted when the key event is received.

properties.name: Relayout
properties.description:
An event for the OnRelayout signal which can be used to subscribe or unsubscribe the event handler.
The OnRelayout signal is emitted after the size has been set on the view during relayout.

properties.name: InterceptTouchEvent
properties.description:
An event for the touched signal which can be used to subscribe or unsubscribe the event handler provided by the user.
The touched signal is emitted when the touch input is received.
This can receive touch events before child.
If it returns false, the child can receive the touch event. If it returns true, the touch event is intercepted. So child cannot receive touch event.

properties.name: DisallowInterceptTouchEvent
properties.description: If child view doesn't want the parent's view to intercept the touch, you can set it to true.         for example :         parent.Add(child);         parent.InterceptTouchEvent += OnInterceptTouchEvent;         View view = child.GetParent() as View;         view.DisallowInterceptTouchEvent = true;         This prevents the parent from intercepting touch.

properties.name: TouchEvent
properties.description:
An event for the touched signal which can be used to subscribe or unsubscribe the event handler provided by the user.
The touched signal is emitted when the touch input is received.

properties.name: HoverEvent
properties.description:
An event for the hovered signal which can be used to subscribe or unsubscribe the event handler provided by the user.
The hovered signal is emitted when the hover input is received.

properties.name: InterceptWheelEvent
properties.description:
An event for the wheel which can be used to subscribe or unsubscribe the event handler provided by the user.
The wheel event is emitted when the wheel input is received.
This can receive wheel events before child.
If it returns false, the child can receive the wheel event. If it returns true, the wheel event is intercepted. So child cannot receive wheel event.

properties.name: DisallowInterceptWheelEvent
properties.description: If child view doesn't want the parent's view to intercept the wheel event, you can set it to true.         for example :         parent.Add(child);         parent.InterceptWheelEvent += OnInterceptWheelEvent;         View view = child.GetParent() as View;         view.DisallowInterceptWheelEvent = true;         This prevents the parent from intercepting wheel event.

properties.name: WheelEvent
properties.description:
An event for the WheelMoved signal which can be used to subscribe or unsubscribe the event handler provided by the user.
The WheelMoved signal is emitted when the wheel event is received.

properties.name: AddedToWindow

An event for the OnWindow signal which can be used to subscribe or unsubscribe the event handler.
The OnWindow signal is emitted after the view has been connected to the window.

properties.name: RemovedFromWindow
properties.description:
An event for the OffWindow signal, which can be used to subscribe or unsubscribe the event handler.
OffWindow signal is emitted after the view has been disconnected from the window.

properties.name: OffScreenRenderingFinished
properties.description: The OffScreenRenderingFinished event is emitted when the off-screen rendering of the view is finished.

properties.name: VisibilityChanged
properties.description:
An event for visibility change which can be used to subscribe or unsubscribe the event handler.
This event is sent when the visibility of this or a parent view is changed.

properties.name: AggregatedVisibilityChanged
properties.description:
An event for aggregated visibility change which can be used to subscribe or unsubscribe the event handler.
This event is sent when visible property of this View, any of its parents (right up to the root layer) or Window changes.

properties.name: LayoutDirectionChanged
properties.description:
Event for layout direction change which can be used to subscribe/unsubscribe the event handler.
This signal is emitted when the layout direction property of this or a parent view is changed.

properties.name: ResourcesLoaded

An event for the ResourcesLoadedSignal signal which can be used to subscribe or unsubscribe the event handler provided by the user.
This signal is emitted after all resources required by a view are loaded and ready.
