properties.name: TouchAreaOffset
properties.description:
The expanded touch area. TouchArea can expand the view's touchable area.
If you set the TouchAreaOffset on an view, when you touch the view, the touch area is used rather than the size of the view.

properties.name: DispatchKeyEvents
properties.description: Gets or sets the status of whether the view should emit key event signals.         If a View's DispatchKeyEvents is set to false, then itself and parents will not receive key event signals.

properties.name: DispatchTouchEvents
properties.description: Gets or sets the status of whether touch events can be dispatched.         If a View's DispatchTouchEvents is set to false, then it's can not will receive touch and parents will not receive a touch event signal either.         This works without adding a TouchEvent callback in the View.         <note>         If the <see cref="Tizen.NUI.BaseComponents.View.Sensitive"/> is a property that determines whether or not to be hittable, then this property prevents the propagation of the hit touch event.         </note>

properties.name: DispatchParentTouchEvents
properties.description: Gets or sets the status of whether touch events can be dispatched to the parent.         If a View's DispatchParentTouchEvents is set to false, then parents will not receive a touch event signal either.         This works without adding a TouchEvent callback in the View.         <note>         If the <see cref="Tizen.NUI.BaseComponents.View.Sensitive"/> is a property that determines whether or not to be hittable, then this property prevents the propagation of the hit touch event.         </note>

properties.name: DispatchHoverEvents
properties.description: Gets or sets the status of whether hover events can be dispatched.         If a View's DispatchHoverEvents is set to false, then it's can not will receive hover event and parents will not receive a hover event signal either.         This works without adding a HoverEvent callback in the View.         <note>         If the <see cref="Tizen.NUI.BaseComponents.View.Sensitive"/> is a property that determines whether or not to be hittable, then this property prevents the propagation of the hit hover event.         </note>

properties.name: DispatchParentHoverEvents
properties.description: Gets or sets the status of whether hover events can be dispatched to the parent.         If a View's DispatchParentHoverEvents is set to false, then parents will not receive a hover event signal either.         This works without adding a HoverEvent callback in the View.         <note>         If the <see cref="Tizen.NUI.BaseComponents.View.Sensitive"/> is a property that determines whether or not to be hittable, then this property prevents the propagation of the hit hover event.         </note>

properties.name: DispatchWheelEvents
properties.description: Gets or sets the status of whether wheel events can be dispatched.         If a View's DispatchWheelEvents is set to false, then it's can not will receive wheel event and parents will not receive a wheel event signal either.         This works without adding a WheelEvent callback in the View.         <note>         If the <see cref="Tizen.NUI.BaseComponents.View.Sensitive"/> is a property that determines whether or not to be hittable, then this property prevents the propagation of the hit hover event.         </note>

properties.name: DispatchParentWheelEvents
properties.description: Gets or sets the status of whether wheel events can be dispatched to the parent.         If a View's DispatchParentWheelEvents is set to false, then parents will not receive a wheel event signal either.         This works without adding a WheelEvent callback in the View.         <note>         If the <see cref="Tizen.NUI.BaseComponents.View.Sensitive"/> is a property that determines whether or not to be hittable, then this property prevents the propagation of the hit hover event.         </note>

properties.name: DispatchGestureEvents
properties.description: Gets or sets the status of whether the view should emit Gesture event signals.         If a View's DispatchGestureEvents is set to false, then itself and parents will not receive all gesture event signals.         The itself and parents does not receive tap, pinch, pan, rotation, or longpress gestures.

properties.name: DispatchParentGestureEvents
properties.description: Gets or sets the status of whether the view should emit Gesture event signals.         If a View's DispatchParentGestureEvents is set to false, then parents will not receive all gesture event signals.         The parents does not receive tap, pinch, pan, rotation, or longpress gestures.

properties.name: DispatchTouchMotion
properties.description: Gets or sets the status of whether motion event of Touch can be dispatched.         If a View's DispatchTouchMotion is set to false, then it's can not will receive motion event of TouchEvent.

properties.name: DispatchHoverMotion
properties.description: Gets or sets the status of whether motion event of Hover can be dispatched.         If a View's DispatchHoverMotion is set to false, then it's can not will receive motion event of HoverEvent.

methods.name: SetSizeModeFactor
methods.description:
Sets the relative to parent size factor of the view.
This factor is only used when ResizePolicy is set to either: ResizePolicy::SIZE_RELATIVE_TO_PARENT or ResizePolicy::SIZE_FIXED_OFFSET_FROM_PARENT.
This view's size is set to the view's size multiplied by or added to this factor, depending on ResizePolicy.

methods.name: GetHeightForWidth
methods.description:
Calculates the height of the view given a width.
The natural size is used for default calculation.
Size 0 is treated as aspect ratio 1:1.

methods.name: GetWidthForHeight
methods.description:
Calculates the width of the view given a height.
The natural size is used for default calculation.
Size 0 is treated as aspect ratio 1:1.

methods.name: GetRelayoutSize

methods.description: Rotate the view look at specific position.         It will change the view's orientation property.

widgets.name: Adaptor
widgets.description: An Adaptor object is used to initialize and control how Dali runs.     It provides the lifecycle interface that allows the application     writer to provide their own main loop and other platform related     features.     The Adaptor class provides a means for initialising the resources required by the Dali::Core.     When dealing with platform events, the application writer must ensure that DALi is called in a     thread-safe manner.     As soon as the Adaptor class is created and started, the application writer can initialise their     view objects straight away or as required by the main loop they intend to use (there is no     need to wait for an initialize signal as per the Tizen.NUI.Application class).

properties.description: An Adaptor object is used to initialize and control how Dali runs.     It provides the lifecycle interface that allows the application     writer to provide their own main loop and other platform related     features.     The Adaptor class provides a means for initialising the resources required by the Dali::Core.     When dealing with platform events, the application writer must ensure that DALi is called in a     thread-safe manner.     As soon as the Adaptor class is created and started, the application writer can initialise their     view objects straight away or as required by the main loop they intend to use (there is no     need to wait for an initialize signal as per the Tizen.NUI.Application class).

widgets.description: Layers provide a mechanism for overlaying groups of actors on top of each other.

properties.name: Viewport
properties.description:
UI control rendering mode (default mode). This mode is designed for UI controls that can overlap. In this mode renderer order will be respective to the tree hierarchy of Actors.
The rendering order is depth first, so for the following actor tree, A will be drawn first, then B, D, E, then C, F. This ensures that overlapping actors are drawn as expected (whereas, with breadth first traversal, the actors would interleave).

widgets.name: Matrix
widgets.description:
The Matrix class represents transformations and projections.
The matrix is stored as a flat array and is Column Major, i.e. the storage order is as follows (numbers represent indices of array):
0 4 8 12 1 5 9 13 2 6 10 14 3 7 11 15 Each axis is contiguous in memory, so the x-axis corresponds to elements 0, 1, 2 and 3, the y-axis corresponds to elements 4, 5, 6, 7, the z-axis corresponds to elements 12, 13, 14 and 15, and the translation vector corresponds to elements 12, 13 and 14.

A 3x3 matrix.
The matrix is stored as a flat array and is Column Major, i.e. the storage order is as follows (numbers represent indices of array):
0 3 6 1 4 7 2 5 8 Each axis is contiguous in memory, so the x-axis corresponds to elements 0, 1, and 2, the y-axis corresponds to elements 3, 4, 5, the z-axis corresponds to elements 6, 7, and 8.

widgets.name: StyleManager
widgets.description:
The StyleManager informs applications of the system theme change, and supports application theme change at runtime.
Applies various styles to controls using the properties system.
On theme change, it automatically updates all controls, then raises a event to inform the application.
If the application wants to customize the theme, RequestThemeChange needs to be called.
It provides the path to the application resource root folder, from there the filename can be specified along with any subfolders, for example, Images, Models, etc.

It provides the path to the application resource root folder, from there the filename can be specified along with any subfolders, for example, Images, Models, etc.

widgets.name: Gesture
widgets.description:
Base structure for different gestures that an application can receive.
A Gesture is an event that is produced from a combination of several touch events in a particular order or within a certain time frame (for example, pinch).

properties.name: GestureType
properties.description:
Base structure for different gestures that an application can receive.
A Gesture is an event that is produced from a combination of several touch events in a particular order or within a certain time frame (for example, pinch).

properties.description: When two touch points move away or towards each other.

properties.description: There is no state associated with this gesture.

properties.name: SourceDataType
properties.description: invalid data.

properties.name: Time
properties.description: invalid data.

widgets.name: GestureDetector
widgets.description:
GestureDetectors analyses a stream of touch events and attempt to determine the intention of the user.
An view is attached to a gesture detector and if the detector recognises a pattern in its analysis, it will trigger a detected event to the application.
This is the base class for different gesture detectors available and provides functionality that is common to all the gesture detectors.

methods.name: Attach
methods.description:
GestureDetectors analyses a stream of touch events and attempt to determine the intention of the user.
An view is attached to a gesture detector and if the detector recognises a pattern in its analysis, it will trigger a detected event to the application.
This is the base class for different gesture detectors available and provides functionality that is common to all the gesture detectors.

methods.name: Detach
methods.description: Detaches the attached view from the gesture detector.

methods.name: DetachAll
methods.description: Detaches all the views that have been attached to the gesture detector.

methods.name: GetAttachedViewCount
methods.description: Returns the number of views attached to the gesture detector.

methods.name: GetAttachedView
methods.description: Returns an view by index. An empty handle if the index is not valid.

methods.name: HandleEvent
methods.description: Handles the event for a given view and touch input.         This method should only be called when SetGeometryHittestEnabled is set to true.         It processes the touch input and attempts to recognize gestures based on the provided view and touch data.

methods.name: CancelAllOtherGestureDetectors
methods.description: Cancels all other gesture detectors that are currently recognizing gestures by HandleEvent(View view, Touch touch) api

widgets.name: LongPressGesture
widgets.description:
A long press gesture is emitted when the user holds the screen with the stated number of fingers.
A long press gesture finishes when all touches have been released.

properties.name: NumberOfTouches
properties.description:
A long press gesture is emitted when the user holds the screen with the stated number of fingers.
A long press gesture finishes when all touches have been released.

properties.name: ScreenPoint
properties.description:
This is the point, in screen coordinates, where the long press occurred.
If a multi-touch long press, then this is the centroid of all the touch points.

properties.name: LocalPoint
properties.description:
This is the point, in local actor coordinates, where the long press occurred.
If a multi-touch long press, then this is the centroid of all the touch points.

widgets.name: LongPressGestureDetector
widgets.description:
This class emits a signals when a long press gesture occurs that meets the requirements set by the application.
For any valid long press, two signals will be emitted:
- First identifying the beginning (state = Started) i.e. when fingers held down for the required time.
- Second identifying the ending (state = Finished) i.e. when fingers are released.

- Second identifying the ending (state = Finished) i.e. when fingers are released.

properties.name: View

The number of touches corresponds to the number of fingers a user has on the screen. The default is 1.

widgets.name: PanGesture
widgets.description:
A PanGesture is emitted when the user moves one or more fingers in a particular direction.
A pan gesture will end in the following ways:
- User releases the primary finger (the first touch).
- User has more fingers on the screen than the maximum specified.
- User has less fingers on the screen than the minimum specified.
- Cancelled by the system.
A pan gesture will continue to be sent to the actor under than initial pan until it ends.

properties.name: Velocity
properties.description:
A PanGesture is emitted when the user moves one or more fingers in a particular direction.
A pan gesture will end in the following ways:
- User releases the primary finger (the first touch).
- User has more fingers on the screen than the maximum specified.
- User has less fingers on the screen than the minimum specified.
- Cancelled by the system.
A pan gesture will continue to be sent to the actor under than initial pan until it ends.

properties.name: Displacement
properties.description:
This is a Vector2 showing how much the user has panned (dragged) since the last pan gesture or, if the gesture has just started, then the amount panned since the user touched the screen.
A positive x value shows that the user is panning to the right, a negative x value means the opposite.
A positive y value shows that the user is panning downwards, a negative y value means upwards.

properties.name: Position
properties.description: The current touch position of the primary touch point in local actor coordinates.

properties.name: ScreenVelocity
properties.description:
The velocity at which the user is moving their fingers.
This is represented as a Vector2 and is the pixel movement per millisecond.
A positive x value shows that the user is panning to the right, a negative x value means the opposite.
A positive y value shows that the user is panning downwards, a negative y values means upwards.
This value represents the screen coordinates.

properties.name: ScreenDisplacement
properties.description:
This is a Vector2 showing how much the user has panned (dragged) since the last pan gesture or, if the gesture has just started, then the amount panned since the user touched the screen.
A positive x value shows that the user is panning to the right, a negative x value means the opposite.
A positive y value shows that the user is panning downwards, a negative y value means upwards.
This value is in screen coordinates.

properties.name: ScreenPosition
properties.description: The current touch position of the primary touch point in screen coordinates.

properties.name: NumberOfTouches
properties.description: The total number of fingers touching the screen in a pan gesture.

methods.name: GetSpeed
methods.description:
A PanGesture is emitted when the user moves one or more fingers in a particular direction.
A pan gesture will end in the following ways:
- User releases the primary finger (the first touch).
- User has more fingers on the screen than the maximum specified.
- User has less fingers on the screen than the minimum specified.
- Cancelled by the system.
A pan gesture will continue to be sent to the actor under than initial pan until it ends.

methods.name: GetDistance
methods.description:
Returns the distance the user has panned (dragged) since the last pan gesture or, if the gesture has just started, then the distance moved since the user touched the screen.
This is always a positive value.

methods.name: GetScreenSpeed
methods.description:
Returns the speed at which the user is moving their fingers relative to screen coordinates.
This is the pixel movement per millisecond.

methods.name: GetScreenDistance
methods.description:
Returns the distance the user has panned (dragged) since the last pan gesture in screen coordinates or, if the gesture has just started, then the distance in screen coordinates moved since the user touched the screen.
This is always a positive value.

methods.name: AddAngle
methods.description:
The pan gesture is only emitted if the pan occurs in the direction specified by this method with a +/- threshold allowance.
If an angle of 0.0 degrees is specified and the threshold is 45 degrees then the acceptable direction range is from -45 to 45 degrees.
The angle added using this API is only checked when the gesture first starts, after that, this detector will emit the gesture regardless of what angle the pan is moving. The user can add as many angles as they require.

methods.name: AddAngle
methods.description:
The pan gesture is only emitted if the pan occurs in the direction specified by this method with a +/- threshold allowance. The default threshold (PI * 0.25) is used.
The angle added using this API is only checked when the gesture first starts, after that, this detector will emit the gesture regardless of what angle the pan is moving.
The user can add as many angles as they require.

methods.name: AddDirection
methods.description:
A helper method for adding bi-directional angles where the pan should take place.
In other words, if 0 is requested, then PI will also be added so that we have both left and right scrolling.

methods.name: AddDirection
methods.description:
A helper method for adding bi-directional angles where the pan should take place. In other words, if 0 is requested, then PI will also be added so that we have both left and right scrolling.
The default threshold (PI * 0.25) is used.

methods.name: GetAngleCount
methods.description: Returns the count of angles that this pan gesture detector emits a signal.

methods.name: ClearAngles
methods.description: Clears any directional angles that are used by the gesture detector.

methods.name: RemoveAngle
methods.description: Removes the angle specified from the container. This will only remove the first instance of the angle found from the container.

methods.name: RemoveDirection
methods.description: Removes the two angles that make up the direction from the container.

widgets.name: Touch
widgets.description:
Touch events are a collection of points at a specific moment in time.
When a multi-touch event occurs, each point represents the points that are currently being touched or the points where a touch has stopped.

properties.name: MouseButton
properties.description:
Touch events are a collection of points at a specific moment in time.
When a multi-touch event occurs, each point represents the points that are currently being touched or the points where a touch has stopped.

methods.name: GetTime
methods.description:
Touch events are a collection of points at a specific moment in time.
When a multi-touch event occurs, each point represents the points that are currently being touched or the points where a touch has stopped.

methods.name: GetPointCount
methods.description: Returns the total number of points in this TouchData.         Point means the touched point.         If there is one touch point, the point index is 0.         If there are two touch points, the point index has 0 and 1.

methods.name: GetDeviceId
methods.description:
Returns the ID of the device used for the point specified.
Each point has a unique device ID, which specifies the device used for that point. This is returned by this method.
If a point is greater than GetPointCount(), then this method will return -1.

methods.name: GetState
methods.description:
Retrieves the state of the point specified.
If a point is greater than GetPointCount(), then this method will return PointState.Finished.

methods.name: GetHitView
methods.description:
Retrieves the view that was underneath the point specified.
If a point is greater than GetPointCount(), then this method will return an empty handle.

methods.name: GetLocalPosition
methods.description:
Retrieves the coordinates relative to the top-left of the hit view at the point specified.
The top-left of an view is (0.0, 0.0, 0.5).
If you require the local coordinates of another view (for example, the parent of the hit view), then you should use ScreenToLocal().
If a point is greater than GetPointCount(), then this method will return Vector2.Zero.

methods.name: GetScreenPosition
methods.description:
Retrieves the coordinates relative to the top-left of the screen of the point specified.
If a point is greater than GetPointCount(), then this method will return Vector2.Zero.

methods.name: GetRadius
methods.description:
Retrieves the radius of the press point.
This is the average of both the horizontal and vertical radii of the press point.
If point is greater than GetPointCount(), then this method will return 0.0f.

methods.name: GetEllipseRadius
methods.description:
Retrieves both the horizontal and the vertical radii of the press point.
If a point is greater than GetPointCount(), then this method will return Vector2.Zero.

methods.name: GetPressure
methods.description:
Retrieves the touch pressure.
The pressure range starts at 0.0f.
Normal pressure is defined as 1.0f.
A value between 0.0f and 1.0f means light pressure has been applied.
A value greater than 1.0f means more pressure than normal has been applied.
If point is greater than GetPointCount(), then this method will return 1.0f.

widgets.name: Wheel
widgets.description:
The wheel event structure is used to store a wheel rolling, it facilitates processing of the wheel rolling and passing to other libraries like Toolkit.
There is a key modifier which relates to keys like Alt, Shift, and Ctrl functions are supplied to check if they have been pressed when the wheel is being rolled.
We support a mouse device and there may be another custom device that support the wheel event. The device type is specified as \e type.
The mouse wheel event can be sent to the specific actor but the custom wheel event will be sent to the window.

We support a mouse device and there may be another custom device that support the wheel event. The device type is specified as \e type.
The mouse wheel event can be sent to the specific actor but the custom wheel event will be sent to the window.

properties.name: Z
properties.description: The offset of rolling (positive value means roll down or clockwise, and negative value means roll up or counter-clockwise).

properties.name: TimeStamp
properties.description: The time the wheel is being rolled.

The mouse wheel event can be sent to the specific actor but the custom wheel event will be sent to the window.

widgets.name: PixelBuffer
widgets.description: The PixelBuffer object holds a pixel buffer.     The PixelBuffer keeps ownership of its initial buffer. However, the     user is free to modify the pixel data, either directly or via image operations.     In order to upload the pixel data to the texture memory, there are two     possibilities, either convert it back to a PixelData object, which     releases the PixelBuffer object, leaving the user with an empty handle     (ideal for one-time indirect image manipulation) or create a new     PixelData object from this object, leaving the buffer intact (ideal     for continuous manipulation).

methods.name: Convert
methods.description: The PixelBuffer object holds a pixel buffer.     The PixelBuffer keeps ownership of its initial buffer. However, the     user is free to modify the pixel data, either directly or via image operations.     In order to upload the pixel data to the texture memory, there are two     possibilities, either convert it back to a PixelData object, which     releases the PixelBuffer object, leaving the user with an empty handle     (ideal for one-time indirect image manipulation) or create a new     PixelData object from this object, leaving the buffer intact (ideal     for continuous manipulation).

methods.name: CreatePixelData
methods.description: Copy the data from this object into a new PixelData object, which could be         used for uploading to a texture.

methods.name: GetWidth
methods.description: Gets the width of the buffer in pixels.

methods.name: GetHeight
methods.description: Gets the height of the buffer in pixels.

methods.name: GetPixelFormat
methods.description: Gets the pixel format.

methods.name: GetStrideBytes
methods.description: Gets the stride of the buffer in bytes. 0 means the buffer is tightly packed

methods.name: ApplyMask
methods.signature: void ApplyMask(PixelBuffer mask, float contentScale, bool cropToMask)
methods.description: Apply the mask to this pixel data and return a new pixel data that contains         the masked image. If this PixelBuffer does not have an alpha channel, then         the resultant PixelBuffer will be converted to a format that supports at         least the width of the color channels and the alpha channel from the mask.         If cropToMask is set to <c>true</c>, then the contentScale is applied first to         this buffer, and the target buffer is cropped to the size of the mask. If         it is set to <c>false</c>, then the mask is scaled to match the size of this buffer         before the mask is applied.

widgets.name: FocusManager
widgets.description:
Provides the functionality of handling keyboard navigation and maintaining the two-dimensional keyboard focus chain.
It provides functionality of setting the focus and moving the focus in four directions( i.e., left, right, up, and down).
It also draws a highlight for the focused view and sends an event when the focus is changed.

properties.name: PreFocusChange
properties.description:
Provides the functionality of handling keyboard navigation and maintaining the two-dimensional keyboard focus chain.
It provides functionality of setting the focus and moving the focus in four directions( i.e., left, right, up, and down).
It also draws a highlight for the focused view and sends an event when the focus is changed.

properties.name: FocusChanging
properties.description:
FocusChanging will be triggered before the focus is going to be changed.
The FocusManager makes the best guess for which view to focus towards the given direction, but applications might want to change that.
By connecting with this event, they can check the proposed view to focus and assign a different view if they wish.
This event is only triggered when the navigation key is pressed and FocusManager tries to move the focus automatically.
It won't be emitted for focus movement by calling the SetCurrentFocusView directly.

properties.name: FocusChanged
properties.description: The FocusChanged will be triggered after the current focused view has been changed.

properties.name: FocusGroupChanged
properties.description:
The FocusGroupChanged will be triggered when the focus group has been changed.
If the current focus group has a parent layout control, the FocusManager will make the best guess for the next focus group to move the focus to in the given direction (forward or backward).
If not, the application has to set the new focus.

properties.name: FocusedViewActivated
properties.description: The FocusedViewActivated will be triggered when the current focused view has the enter key pressed on it.

properties.name: FocusedViewEnterKeyPressed
properties.description: [Obsolete("Do not use this, that will be deprecated.")]

properties.name: ICustomFocusAlgorithm
properties.description:
ICustomFocusAlgorithm is used to provide the custom keyboard focus algorithm for retrieving the next focusable view.
The application can implement the interface and override the keyboard focus behavior.
If the focus is changing within a layout container, then the layout container is queried first to provide the next focusable view.
If this does not provide a valid view, then the Keyboard FocusManager will check focusable properties to determine the next focusable actor.
If focusable properties are not set, then the keyboard FocusManager calls the GetNextFocusableView() method of this interface.

properties.name: FocusGroupLoop
properties.description: Get the next focus actor.

properties.name: FocusIndicator
properties.description:
Gets or sets the focus indicator view.
This will replace the default focus indicator view in the FocusManager and will be added to the focused view as a highlight.

properties.name: ClearFocusOnWindowFocusLost
properties.description: Gets or sets whether to clear focus when window loses focus.         By default, this is enabled.         When disabled, the focus state is preserved even when the window loses focus.

properties.name: Instance
properties.description: Gets the singleton of the FocusManager object.

properties.name: CurrentView
properties.description:
Moves the keyboard focus to the given view.
Only one view can be focused at the same time.
The view must be in the stage already and keyboard focusable.

properties.name: ProposedView
properties.description: The  proposed view.

properties.name: CurrentView
properties.description: The focus move direction.

properties.name: NextView
properties.description: The next focus view.

properties.name: Previous
properties.description: The previously focused view.

properties.name: Current
properties.description: The current focused view after focus changed.

properties.name: CurrentView
properties.description: Event arguments that passed via the FocusGroupChanged signal.

methods.name: SetCurrentFocusView
methods.description:
Provides the functionality of handling keyboard navigation and maintaining the two-dimensional keyboard focus chain.
It provides functionality of setting the focus and moving the focus in four directions( i.e., left, right, up, and down).
It also draws a highlight for the focused view and sends an event when the focus is changed.

methods.name: GetCurrentFocusView
methods.description: Gets the current focused view.

methods.name: MoveFocus
methods.description: Moves the focus to the next focusable view in the focus chain in the given direction (according to the focus traversal order).

methods.name: ClearFocus
methods.description:
Clears the focus from the current focused view if any, so that no view is focused in the focus chain.
It will emit the FocusChanged event without the current focused view.

methods.name: MoveFocusBackward
methods.description: Move the focus to previous focused view.

methods.name: SetAsFocusGroup
methods.description:
Sets whether the view is a focus group that can limit the scope of the focus movement to its child views in the focus chain.
Layout controls set themselves as focus groups by default.

methods.name: IsFocusGroup
methods.description: Checks whether the view is set as a focus group or not.

methods.name: GetFocusGroup
methods.description: Returns the closest ancestor of the given view that is a focus group.

methods.name: SetCustomAlgorithm
methods.description: Provides the implementation of a custom focus algorithm interface to allow the application to define the focus logic.

methods.name: EnableDefaultAlgorithm
methods.description:
Sets to use the automatic focus moveing algorithm.
It moves the focus to the view closest to the keyboard movement direction.

methods.name: IsDefaultAlgorithmEnabled
methods.description: Checks default focus moveing algorithm is enabled or not

methods.name: GetNearestFocusableActor
methods.signature: View GetNearestFocusableActor(View rootView, View focusedView, View.FocusDirection direction)
methods.description: Get the nearest focusable view.

methods.name: SetFocusFinderRootView
methods.description: Sets the root view to start moving focus when DefaultAlgorithm is enabled.         This will only look for focusable Views within that View tree when looking for the next focus.

methods.name: ResetFocusFinderRootView
methods.description: Reset the root view that starts moving focus when DefaultAlgorithm is enabled.         When reset, the window becomes root.

methods.name: EnableFocusIndicator
methods.description: Decide using focus indicator or not

methods.name: IsFocusIndicatorEnabled
methods.description: Check focus indicator is enabled or not

methods.name: GetDefaultFocusIndicator
methods.description: Get a default focus indicator

methods.name: GetLastFocusChangeDevice
methods.description: Gets the device of the last focus change.         This method returns what caused the most recent focus change,         allowing applications to differentiate between different input methods.

methods.name: GetLastFocusChangeDeviceName
methods.description: Gets the device name that caused the last focus change.         This method returns the name of the input device that caused         the most recent focus change. For non-device inputs (like programmatic focus changes), an empty string may be returned.

methods.description: Do not use this, that will be deprecated.

methods.name: Activate
methods.description:
Activates the IMF.
It means that the text editing has started.
If the hardware keyboard is not connected, then it shows the virtual keyboard.

methods.name: Deactivate
methods.description:
Deactivates the IMF.
It means that the text editing is complete.

methods.name: RestoreAfterFocusLost
methods.description:
Gets the restoration status, which controls if the keyboard is restored after the focus is lost and then regained.
If true, then the keyboard will be restored (activated) after the focus is regained.

methods.name: SetRestoreAfterFocusLost
methods.description: Sets the status whether the IMF has to restore the keyboard after losing focus.

methods.description: Sends a message reset to the pre-edit state or the IMF module.

widgets.name: FlexLayout
widgets.description: This class implements a flex layout.     The flex layout implementation is based on open source Facebook Yoga layout engine.     For more information about the flex layout API and how to use it please refer to https://yogalayout.com/docs/     We implement the subset of the API in the class below.

properties.name: Direction
properties.description: This class implements a flex layout.     The flex layout implementation is based on open source Facebook Yoga layout engine.     For more information about the flex layout API and how to use it please refer to https://yogalayout.com/docs/     We implement the subset of the API in the class below.

properties.name: Justification
properties.description:
Gets/Sets the justification in the layout. Justify content describes how to align children within the main axis of their container.
For example, you can use this property to center a child horizontally within a container with set to or vertically within a container with set to .

properties.name: WrapType
properties.description:
Gets/Sets the wrap in the layout. The flex wrap property is set on containers and controls what happens when children overflow the size of the container along the main axis.
By default children are forced into a single line (which can shrink elements).
If wrapping is allowed items are wrapped into multiple lines along the main axis if needed. wrap reverse behaves the same, but the order of the lines is reversed.
When wrapping lines can be used to specify how the lines are placed in the container.

properties.name: Alignment
properties.description:
Gets/Sets the alignment of the layout content. Alignment defines the distribution of lines along the cross-axis.
This only has effect when items are wrapped to multiple lines using flex wrap.

properties.name: ItemsAlignment
properties.description:
Gets/Sets the alignment of the layout items. Items alignment describes how to align children along the cross axis of their container.
Align items is very similar to but instead of applying to the main axis, align items applies to the cross axis.

properties.name: FlexDirection
properties.description: Enumeration for the direction of the main axis in the flex container.         This determines the direction that flex items are laid out in the flex container.

properties.name: FlexJustification
properties.description: The flexible items are displayed vertically as a column

properties.name: FlexWrapType
properties.description: Items are positioned at the beginning of the container.

properties.name: AlignmentType
properties.description: Flex items laid out in single line (shrunk to fit the flex container along the main axis)

properties.name: PositionType
properties.description: Inherits the same alignment from the parent

properties.name: FlexAlignmentSelf
properties.description: Flex items laid out relatively.

properties.name: FlexAspectRatio
properties.description: Gets or sets the aspect ratio of the flex layout item.

properties.name: FlexBasis
properties.description: Gets or sets the basis of the flex layout item.

properties.name: FlexShrink
properties.description: Gets or sets the shrink of the flex layout item.

properties.name: FlexGrow
properties.description: Gets or sets the grow of the flex layout item.

properties.name: FlexItem
properties.description: Gets or sets the item handle of the flex layout item.

properties.name: MarkDirty
properties.description: Gets or sets the mark dirty of the flex layout item.

methods.name: GetFlexAlignmentSelf
methods.description: This class implements a flex layout.     The flex layout implementation is based on open source Facebook Yoga layout engine.     For more information about the flex layout API and how to use it please refer to https://yogalayout.com/docs/     We implement the subset of the API in the class below.

methods.name: GetFlexPositionType
methods.description: Gets the position type of the child view.

methods.name: GetFlexAspectRatio
methods.description: Gets the aspect ratio of the child view.

methods.name: GetFlexBasis
methods.description: Gets the basis of the child view.

methods.name: GetFlexShrink
methods.description: Gets the shrink of the child view.

methods.name: GetFlexGrow
methods.description: Gets the grow of the child view.

methods.name: SetFlexAlignmentSelf
methods.description:
Sets the alignment self of the child view.
Alignment self has the same options and effect as but instead of affecting the children within a container, you can apply this property to a single child to change its alignment within its parent.
Alignment self overrides any option set by the parent with .

methods.name: SetFlexPositionType
methods.description:
Sets the position type of the child view.
The position type of an element defines how it is positioned within its parent. By default a child is positioned relatively. This means a child is positioned according to the normal flow of the layout, and then offset relative to that position based on the values of .
When positioned absolutely an element doesn't take part in the normal layout flow. It is instead laid out independent of its siblings. The position is determined based on the .

methods.name: SetFlexAspectRatio
methods.description:
Sets the aspect ratio of the child view. Aspect ratio Defines as the ratio between the width and the height of a node e.g. if a node has an aspect ratio of 2 then its width is twice the size of its height.
Aspect ratio accepts any floating point value > 0. this has higher priority than flex grow.

methods.name: SetFlexBasis
methods.description:
Sets the flex basis of the child view. Flex basis is an axis-independent way of providing the default size of an item along the main axis.
Setting the flex basis of a child is similar to setting the width of that child if its parent is a container with or setting the height of a child if its parent is a container with .
The flex basis of an item is the default size of that item, the size of the item before any flex grow and flex shrink calculations are performed.

methods.name: SetFlexShrink
methods.description:
Sets the flex shrink of the child view. Flex shrink describes how to shrink children along the main axis in the case that the total size of the children overflow the size of the container on the main axis.
Flex shrink is very similar to flex grow and can be thought of in the same way if any overflowing size is considered to be negative remaining space. These two properties also work well together by allowing children to grow and shrink as needed.
Flex shrink accepts any floating point value >= 0, with 1 being the default value. A container will shrink its children weighted by the child's flex shrink value.

methods.name: SetFlexGrow
methods.description:
Sets the grow of the child view. Flex grow describes how any space within a container should be distributed among its children along the main axis. After laying out its children, a container will distribute any remaining space according to the flex grow values specified by its children.
Flex grow accepts any floating point value >= 0, with 0 being the default value.
A container will distribute any remaining space among its children weighted by the child's flex grow value.

methods.name: Layout
methods.signature: void Layout(LayoutLength left, LayoutLength top, LayoutLength right, LayoutLength bottom)
methods.description:
Assign a size and position to a layout and all of its descendants.
This is the second phase of the layout mechanism. (The first is measuring). In this phase, each parent calls layout on all of its children to position them. This is typically done using the child
measurements that were stored in the measure pass.

methods.description:

measurements that were stored in the measure pass.

methods.name: IsPaddingHandledByNative
methods.description: Indicates if padding is handled by native.         By default, padding is not handled by native if layout is set to view.         Instead, padding is handled by layout if layout is set to view.         If padding is not handled by native, then view padding is copied to layout padding and         view padding is initialized to zero not to make native handle padding.         If padding is handled by native, then view padding is preserved and padding is handled         by native.

widgets.name: MaskEffect
widgets.description:
Mask effect of a View. MaskEffect uses the target's camera to render both target and source.
To apply the mask correctly, align the source's size and position with the target.
Use 'targetMaskOnce' / 'sourceMaskOnce' to skip re-rendering static inputs.
 Applications can apply MaskEffect as the example below : MaskEffect effect = new MaskEffect(); view.SetRenderEffect(effect);

properties.name: TargetMaskOnce
properties.description:
Mask effect of a View. MaskEffect uses the target's camera to render both target and source.
To apply the mask correctly, align the source's size and position with the target.
Use 'targetMaskOnce' / 'sourceMaskOnce' to skip re-rendering static inputs.
 Applications can apply MaskEffect as the example below : MaskEffect effect = new MaskEffect(); view.SetRenderEffect(effect);

widgets.name: Theme
widgets.description: <para>     Basically, the Theme is a dictionary of <seealso cref="ViewStyle"/>s that can decorate NUI <seealso cref="View"/>s.     Each ViewStyle item is identified by a string key that can be matched the <seealso cref="View.StyleName"/>.     </para>     <para>     The main purpose of providing theme is to separate style details from the structure.     Managing style separately makes it easier to customize the look of application by user context.     Also since a theme can be created from xaml file, it can be treated as a resource.     This enables sharing styles with other applications.     </para>

widgets.name: ScaleTransition
widgets.description: ScaleTransition provides smoothly appearing/disappearing scale effects for target View.     If this transition is for appearing, the View comes out with the scale factor applied     and will be animated to its original scale.     If this transition is for disappearing, the View starts at its original size     and will finally become scaled by scale factor and vanishes.

properties.name: ScaleFactor
properties.description: ScaleTransition provides smoothly appearing/disappearing scale effects for target View.     If this transition is for appearing, the View comes out with the scale factor applied     and will be animated to its original scale.     If this transition is for disappearing, the View starts at its original size     and will finally become scaled by scale factor and vanishes.

widgets.name: SlideTransition
widgets.description: SlideTransition class is a cluster of properties for the slide transition of a View.     SlideTransition provides smoothly appearing/disappearing effects for target Control.     The direction the target Control is comming from or going to can be selected in the pre-defined directions at the SlideTransitionDirection {UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT}     And, to use custom direction, the direction can be set by using Vector2.

properties.name: Direction
properties.description: SlideTransition class is a cluster of properties for the slide transition of a View.     SlideTransition provides smoothly appearing/disappearing effects for target Control.     The direction the target Control is comming from or going to can be selected in the pre-defined directions at the SlideTransitionDirection {UP, DOWN, LEFT, RIGHT}     And, to use custom direction, the direction can be set by using Vector2.

properties.name: UseGroupTimePeriod
properties.description: TransitionGroup class is a cluster of properties to use multiple Transitions on a target.     FadeTransition, ScaleTransition, and SlideTransition can be added on this group with AddTransition method.     The transitions can be started at the same time or can be started sequentially in order.

properties.name: StepTransition
properties.description: Set/Get whether the child Transitions are started sequentially or not.         If StepTransition is true, the child Transitions starts sequentially with an interval of "TimePeriod.DurationMilliseconds/#ofChildTransitions".

widgets.name: Capture
widgets.description: Capture snapshots the current scene and save as a file. Applications should follow the example below to create capture : Capture capture = new Capture(); If required, you can also subscribe Finished event : capture.Finished += onCaptureFinished; At the subcribed event handler, user can know whether capture finish succeeded state. private void onCaptureFinished(object sender, CaptureFinishedEventArgs e) { if(e.Success) { //capture success, do something. } else { //capture failure, do something. } } suppose that we want to capture View 'A'. And, the View 'A' is overlapped by another View 'B' that is not a child of 'A'. in this case, if source is root of scene, the captured image includes a part of View 'B' on the 'A'. however, if source is just View 'A', the result includes only 'A'.

widgets.description: FontClient provides access to font information and resources.

properties.description: This is used to pre-cache FontConfig in order to improve the runtime performance of the application.

methods.name: FontPreLoad
methods.signature: void FontPreLoad(List<string> fontPathList, List<string> memoryFontPathList, bool useThread, bool syncCreation = true)
methods.description:
This is used to pre-load FreeType font face in order to improve the runtime performance of the application. The fonts in the fontPathList perform FT_New_Face during pre-loading, which can provide some performace benefits.
The fonts in the memoryFontPathList read the font file and cache the buffer in memory during pre-load.
This enables the use of FT_New_Memory_Face during runtime and provides a performance boost.
It requires memory equivalent to the size of each font file.

methods.name: EnableDesignCompatibility
methods.description: Enable font specific design compatibility mode globally.         <remarks>         When enabled, FontMetrics will adjust ascender/descender values at construction         to ensure design compatibility rules.         </remarks>         <note>         This is a one-way switch. Once enabled, it cannot be disabled again during the lifetime of the process.         This function must be called before any text is rendered.         </note>

widgets.name: Timer
widgets.description:
Mechanism to issue simple periodic or one-shot events.
Timer is provided for application developers to be able to issue simple periodic or one-shot events. Please note that the timer callback functions should return as soon as possible because they block the next SignalTick. Please note that timer signals are not in sync with DALi's render timer.
This class is a handle class so it can be stack allocated and used as a member.

widgets.name: BorderVisual
widgets.description: A class encapsulating the property map of the border visual.

properties.name: Color
properties.description: A class encapsulating the property map of the border visual.

properties.name: BorderSize
properties.description:
Gets or sets the width of the border (in pixels).
Mandatory.

properties.name: AntiAliasing
properties.description:
Gets or sets whether the anti-aliasing of the border is required.
If not supplied, the default is false.
Optional.

widgets.name: ColorVisual
widgets.description: A class encapsulating the property map of the color visual.

properties.name: Color
properties.description: A class encapsulating the property map of the color visual.

properties.name: RenderIfTransparent
properties.description: Gets or sets whether to render if the MixColor is transparent.         By default it is false, which means that the ColorVisual will not render if the MIX_COLOR is transparent.

widgets.name: GradientVisual
widgets.description: A class encapsulating the property map of the gradient visual.

properties.name: StartPosition
properties.description: A class encapsulating the property map of the gradient visual.

properties.name: EndPosition
properties.description:
Gets or sets the end position of a linear gradient.
Mandatory for linear.

properties.name: Center
properties.description:
Gets or sets the center point of a radial gradient.
Mandatory for radial and conic.

properties.name: Radius
properties.description:
Gets or sets the size of the radius of a radial gradient.
Mandatory for radial.

properties.name: StopOffset
properties.description:
Gets or sets all the stop offsets.
A PropertyArray of float.
If not supplied, the default is 0.0f and 1.0f.
Optional.

properties.name: StopColor
properties.description:
Gets or sets the color at the stop offsets.
A PropertyArray of color.
At least 2 values are required to show a gradient.
Mandatory.

properties.name: Units
properties.description:
Gets or sets descriptions of the coordinate system for certain attributes of the points in a gradient.
If not supplied, the default is GradientVisualUnitsType.ObjectBoundingBox.
Optional.

properties.name: SpreadMethod
properties.description:
Gets or sets indications of what happens if the gradient starts or ends inside the bounds of the target rectangle.
If not supplied, the default is GradientVisualSpreadMethodType.Pad.
Optional.

properties.name: StartOffset
properties.description:
Gets or sets the gradient's start position offset.
If not supplied, the default is 0.0f.
Optional.

properties.name: StartAngle
properties.description:
Gets or sets the start angle of the conic gradient.
Mandatory for conic.
Optional.

widgets.name: ImageVisual
widgets.description: A class encapsulating the property map of the image visual.

properties.name: URL
properties.description: A class encapsulating the property map of the image visual.

properties.name: AlphaMaskURL
properties.description:
Gets or sets the URL of the alpha mask.
Optional.

properties.name: AuxiliaryImageURL
properties.description: Overlays the auxiliary image on top of an NPatch image.         The resulting visual image will be at least as large as the smallest possible n-patch or the auxiliary image, whichever is larger.

properties.name: FittingMode
properties.description:
Gets or sets fitting options used when resizing images to fit the desired dimensions.
If not supplied, the default is FittingModeType.ShrinkToFit.
For normal quad images only.
Optional.

properties.name: SamplingMode
properties.description:
Gets or sets filtering options used when resizing images to the sample original pixels.
If not supplied, the default is SamplingModeType.Box.
For normal quad images only.
Optional.

properties.name: DesiredWidth
properties.description:
Gets or sets the desired image width.
If not specified, the actual image width is used.
For normal quad images only.
Optional.

properties.name: DesiredHeight
properties.description:
Gets or sets the desired image height.
If not specified, the actual image height is used.
For normal quad images only.
Optional.

properties.name: SynchronousLoading
properties.description:
Gets or sets whether to load the image synchronously.
If not specified, the default is false, i.e., the image is loaded asynchronously.
For normal quad images only.
Optional.

properties.name: BorderOnly
properties.description:
Gets or sets whether to draw the borders only (If true).
If not specified, the default is false.
For n-patch images only.
Optional.

properties.name: PixelArea
properties.description:
Gets or sets the image area to be displayed.
It is a rectangular area.
The first two elements indicate the top-left position of the area, and the last two elements are the areas of the width and the height respectively.
If not specified, the default value is Vector4 (0.0, 0.0, 1.0, 1.0), i.e., the entire area of the image.
For normal quad images only.
Optional.

properties.name: WrapModeU
properties.description:
Gets or sets the wrap mode for the u coordinate.
It decides how the texture should be sampled when the u coordinate exceeds the range of 0.0 to 1.0.
If not specified, the default is WrapModeType.Default(CLAMP).
For normal quad images only.
Optional.

properties.name: WrapModeV
properties.description:
Gets or sets the wrap mode for the v coordinate.
It decides how the texture should be sampled when the v coordinate exceeds the range of 0.0 to 1.0.
The first two elements indicate the top-left position of the area, and the last two elements are the areas of the width and the height respectively.
If not specified, the default is WrapModeType.Default(CLAMP).
For normal quad images only. Optional.

properties.name: MaskContentScale
properties.description: Gets or sets scale factor to apply to the content image before masking.

properties.name: CropToMask
properties.description: Whether to crop image to mask or scale mask to fit image.

properties.name: AuxiliaryImageAlpha
properties.description: An alpha value for mixing between the masked main NPatch image and the auxiliary image.

properties.name: ReleasePolicy
properties.description:
Gets or sets the Image Visual release policy.
It decides if a texture should be released from the cache or kept to reduce the loading time.

properties.name: LoadPolicy
properties.description:
Gets or sets the Image Visual image loading policy.
It decides if a texture should be loaded immediately after source set or only after the visual is added to the window.

properties.name: OrientationCorrection
properties.description:
Gets or sets whether to automatically correct the orientation based on the Exchangeable Image File (EXIF) data.
If not specified, the default is true.
For JPEG images only.
Optional.

properties.name: Atlasing
properties.description: Whether to use the texture atlas or not.         Optional. By default atlasing is off.

widgets.name: MeshVisual
widgets.description: A class encapsulating the property map of the mesh visual.

properties.name: ObjectURL
properties.description: A class encapsulating the property map of the mesh visual.

properties.name: MaterialURL
properties.description:
Gets or sets the location of the ".mtl" file.
If not specified, then a textureless object is assumed.
Optional.

properties.name: MaterialtURL
properties.description:
Gets or sets the location of the ".mtl" file.
If not specified, then a textureless object is assumed.
Optional.

properties.name: TexturesPath
properties.description:
Gets or sets the path to the directory the textures (including gloss and normal) are stored in.
Mandatory if using material.

properties.name: ShadingMode
properties.description:
Gets or sets the type of shading mode that the mesh will use.
If anything the specified shading mode requires is missing, a simpler mode that can be handled with what has been supplied will be used instead.
If not specified, it will use the best it can support (will try MeshVisualShadingModeValue.TexturedWithDetailedSpecularLighting first).
Optional.

properties.name: UseMipmapping
properties.description:
Gets or sets whether to use mipmaps for textures or not.
If not specified, the default is true.
Optional.

properties.name: UseSoftNormals
properties.description:
Gets or sets whether to average normals at each point to smooth textures or not.
If not specified, the default is true.
Optional.

properties.name: LightPosition
properties.description:
Gets or sets the position, in the stage space, of the point light that applies lighting to the model.
This is based off the stage's dimensions, so using the width and the height of the stage halved will correspond to the center, and using all zeroes will place the light at the top-left corner.
If not specified, the default is an offset outwards from the center of the screen.
Optional.

widgets.name: NPatchVisual
widgets.description: A class encapsulating the property map of the n-patch image visual.

properties.name: URL
properties.description: A class encapsulating the property map of the n-patch image visual.

properties.name: BorderOnly
properties.description:
Gets or sets whether to draw the borders only (If true).
If not specified, the default is false.
For n-patch images only.
Optional.

properties.name: Border
properties.description:
The border of the image is in the order: left, right, bottom, top.
For n-patch images only.
Optional.

widgets.name: PrimitiveVisual
widgets.description: A class encapsulating the property map of the primitive visual.

properties.name: Shape
properties.description: A class encapsulating the property map of the primitive visual.

properties.name: MixColor
properties.description:
Gets or sets the color of the shape.
If not specified, the default is Color (0.5, 0.5, 0.5, 1.0).
Applies to all shapes.
Optional.

properties.name: Slices
properties.description:
Gets or sets the number of slices as you go around the shape.
For spheres and conical frustums, this determines how many divisions there are as you go around the object.
If not specified, the default is 128.
The range is from 1 to 255.
Optional.

properties.name: Stacks
properties.description:
Gets or sets the number of stacks as you go down the shape.
For spheres, 'stacks' determines how many layers there are as you go down the object.
If not specified, the default is 128.
The range is from 1 to 255.
Optional.

properties.name: ScaleTopRadius
properties.description:
Gets or sets the scale of the radius of the top circle of a conical frustum.
If not specified, the default is 1.0f.
Applies to: - PrimitiveVisualShapeType.ConicalFrustrum
Only values greater than or equal to 0.0f are accepted.
Optional.

properties.name: ScaleBottomRadius
properties.description:
Gets or sets the scale of the radius of the bottom circle of a conical frustum.
If not specified, the default is 1.5f.
Applies to: - PrimitiveVisualShapeType.ConicalFrustrum
- PrimitiveVisualShapeType.Cone
Only values greater than or equal to 0.0f are accepted.
Optional.

properties.name: ScaleHeight
properties.description:
Gets or sets the scale of the height of a conic.
If not specified, the default is 3.0f.
Applies to:
- PrimitiveVisualShapeType.ConicalFrustrum
- PrimitiveVisualShapeType.Cone
- PrimitiveVisualShapeType.Cylinder
Only values greater than or equal to 0.0f are accepted.
Optional.

properties.name: ScaleRadius
properties.description:
Gets or sets the scale of the radius of a cylinder.
If not specified, the default is 1.0f.
Applies to:
- PrimitiveVisualShapeType.Cylinder
Only values greater than or equal to 0.0f are accepted.
Optional.

properties.name: ScaleDimensions
properties.description:
Gets or sets the dimensions of a cuboid. Scales in the same fashion as a 9-patch image.
If not specified, the default is Vector3.One.
Applies to:
- PrimitiveVisualShapeType.Cube
- PrimitiveVisualShapeType.Octahedron
- PrimitiveVisualShapeType.BevelledCube
Each Vector3 parameter should be greater than or equal to 0.0f.
Optional.

properties.name: BevelPercentage
properties.description:
Gets or sets determines how bevelled the cuboid should be, based off the smallest dimension.
Bevel percentage ranges from 0.0 to 1.0. It affects the ratio of the outer face widths to the width of the overall cube.
If not specified, the default is 0.0f (no bevel).
Applies to:
- PrimitiveVisualShapeType.BevelledCube
The range is from 0.0f to 1.0f.
Optional.

properties.name: BevelSmoothness
properties.description:
Gets or sets descriptions of how smooth the bevelled edges should be.
If not specified, the default is 0.0f (sharp edges).
Applies to:
- PrimitiveVisualShapeType.BevelledCube
The range is from 0.0f to 1.0f.
Optional.

properties.name: LightPosition
properties.description:
Gets or sets the position, in the stage space, of the point light that applies lighting to the model.
This is based off the stage's dimensions, so using the width and the height of the stage halved will correspond to the center, and using all zeroes will place the light at the top-left corner.
If not specified, the default is an offset outwards from the center of the screen.
Applies to all shapes.
Optional.

widgets.name: VisualBase
widgets.description:
Sets whether the actor should be focusable by keyboard navigation.
Visuals reuse geometry, shader etc. across controls. They ensure that the renderer and texture sets exist only when control is on window.
Each visual also responds to actor size and color change, and provides clipping at the renderer level.

properties.name: Name
properties.description:
Sets whether the actor should be focusable by keyboard navigation.
Visuals reuse geometry, shader etc. across controls. They ensure that the renderer and texture sets exist only when control is on window.
Each visual also responds to actor size and color change, and provides clipping at the renderer level.

properties.name: DepthIndex
properties.description: The depth index of this visual.

properties.name: Creation
properties.description: Creates the property map representing this visual.

methods.name: SetTransformAndSize
methods.description:
Sets whether the actor should be focusable by keyboard navigation.
Visuals reuse geometry, shader etc. across controls. They ensure that the renderer and texture sets exist only when control is on window.
Each visual also responds to actor size and color change, and provides clipping at the renderer level.

methods.name: GetHeightForWidth
methods.description: Returns the height for a given width.

methods.name: GetWidthForHeight
methods.description: Returns the width for a given height.

methods.name: GetNaturalSize
methods.description:
Returns the natural size of the visual.
Deriving classes stipulate the natural size and by default a visual has a zero natural size.
A visual may not actually have a natural size until it has been placed on window and acquired all it's resources.

widgets.name: VisualFactory
widgets.description: The VisualFactory is a singleton object that provides and shares visuals between views.

properties.name: Instance
properties.description: The VisualFactory is a singleton object that provides and shares visuals between views.

properties.name: DefaultCreationOptions
properties.description: Do not use this, that will be deprecated. Use VisualFactory.Instance instead.

methods.name: Get
methods.description: The VisualFactory is a singleton object that provides and shares visuals between views.

methods.name: CreateVisual
methods.description: Default creation options for the visual factory.

methods.name: UsePreCompiledShader
methods.description: Compile the visual shader in advance. Afterwards,         when a visual using a new shader is requested, the pre-compiled shader is used.

methods.name: AddPrecompileShader
methods.description: Adds a list of pre-compiled shaders to the visual factory.

widgets.name: VisualMap
widgets.description: A class encapsulating the transform map of the visual.

properties.name: Size
properties.description: A class encapsulating the transform map of the visual.

properties.name: Position
properties.description:
Gets or sets the offset of the visual.
It can be either relative (percentage of the parent) or absolute (in world units).
Optional.

properties.name: RelativeSize
properties.description:
Gets or sets the relative size of the visual
(percentage [0.0f to 1.0f] of the control).
Optional.

properties.name: RelativePosition
properties.description:
Gets or sets the relative offset of the visual
(percentage [0.0f to 1.0f] of the control).
Optional.

properties.name: PositionPolicy
properties.description:
Gets or sets whether the x and y offset values are relative
(percentage [0.0f to 1.0f] of the control) or absolute (in world units).
By default, both the x and the y offset are relative.
Optional.

properties.name: PositionPolicyX
properties.description:
Gets or sets whether the x offset values are relative
(percentage [0.0f to 1.0f] of the control) or absolute (in world units).
By default, the x offset is relative.
Optional.

properties.name: PositionPolicyY
properties.description:
Gets or sets whether the y offset values are relative
(percentage [0.0f to 1.0f] of the control) or absolute (in world units).
By default, the y offset is relative.
Optional.

properties.name: SizePolicy
properties.description:
Gets or sets whether the size values of the width or the height are relative
(percentage [0.0f to 1.0f] of the control) or absolute (in world units).
By default, offsets of both the width and the height are relative to the control's size.
Optional.

properties.name: SizePolicyWidth
properties.description:
Gets or sets whether size values of the width are relative.
(percentage [0.0f to 1.0f] of the control) or absolute (in world units).
By default, the value of the width is relative to the control's width.
Optional.

properties.name: SizePolicyHeight
properties.description:
Gets or sets whether size values of the height are relative
(percentage [0.0f to 1.0f] of the control) or absolute (in world units).
By default, the height value is relative to the control's height.
Optional.

properties.name: DepthIndex
properties.description:
Gets or sets the origin of the visual within its control area.
By default, the origin is AlignType.TopBegin.
Optional.

properties.name: OutputTransformMap
properties.description: Gets the transform map used by the visual.

properties.name: OutputVisualMap
properties.description: Gets the property map to create the visual.

properties.name: Shader
properties.description: The shader to use in the visual.

properties.name: PremultipliedAlpha
properties.description:
Enables or disables the premultiplied alpha.
The premultiplied alpha is false by default unless this behavior is modified by the derived visual type.

properties.name: MixColor
properties.description: Mix color is a blend color for any visual.

properties.name: Opacity
properties.description: Opacity is the alpha component of the mix color discussed above.

properties.name: VisualFittingMode
properties.description: The fitting mode of the visual.         The default is defined by the type of visual (if it is suitable to be stretched or not).

properties.name: CornerRadius
properties.description: The corner radius of the visual.

properties.name: CornerRadiusPolicy
properties.description: Whether the CornerRadius property value is relative (percentage [0.0f to 0.5f] of the view size) or absolute (in world units).         It is absolute by default.         When the policy is relative, the corner radius is relative to the smaller of the view's width and height.

properties.name: BorderlineWidth
properties.description: The borderline width of the visual.

properties.name: BorderlineColor
properties.description: The borderline color of the visual.

properties.name: BorderlineOffset
properties.description: The borderline offset of the visual.

methods.name: Dispose
methods.description: A class encapsulating the transform map of the visual.

properties.description: The text visual with advanced options.

properties.name: ResourceUrlList
properties.description: The visual which can display and control an animated image resource.     We can also set image sequences by using ResourceUrlList and FrameDelay property.

properties.name: FrameDelay
properties.description: The number of milliseconds between each frame in the Image-Array animation.

properties.name: LoopCount
properties.description: Gets and sets the number of times the AnimatedImageVisual will be looped.         The default is -1. If the number is less than 0 then it loops unlimited,otherwise loop loopCount times.

properties.name: FrameSpeedFactor
properties.description: Sets or gets the stop behavior.

properties.name: TotalFrame
properties.description: Get the number of total frames.         Or -1 if image is invalid, or not loaded yet.

properties.name: CurrentFrame
properties.description: Set or get the current frame. When setting a specific frame, it is displayed as a still image.

properties.name: BatchSize
properties.description: Gets and Sets the batch size for pre-loading images in the AnimatedImageVisual. (Advanced)

properties.name: CacheSize
properties.description: Gets and Sets the cache size for loading images in the AnimatedImageVisual. (Advanced)

properties.name: Finished
properties.description: Play the animated image.

events.name: Finished
events.description: The visual which can display and control an animated image resource.     We can also set image sequences by using ResourceUrlList and FrameDelay property.

methods.name: Play
methods.description: The visual which can display and control an animated image resource.     We can also set image sequences by using ResourceUrlList and FrameDelay property.

methods.description: Pause the animated image.
