widgets.name: BorderVisual
widgets.description: Simple visual to render a solid border.

properties.name: BorderWidth
properties.description: Simple visual to render a solid border.

properties.name: AntiAliasing
properties.description: Gets or sets whether the anti-aliasing of the border is required. default is false.

widgets.name: ColorVisual
widgets.description: Simple visual to render a solid color.

properties.name: BlurRadius
properties.description: Simple visual to render a solid color.

properties.name: CutoutPolicy
properties.description: Cutout policy of color visual

properties.name: CornerRadius
properties.description: The radius for the rounded corners of the visual.         The values in Vector4 are used in clockwise order from top-left to bottom-left : Vector4(top-left-corner, top-right-corner, bottom-right-corner, bottom-left-corner).         Each radius will clamp internally to the half of smaller of the visual's width or height.

properties.name: CornerRadiusPolicy
properties.description: Whether the CornerRadius property value is relative (percentage [0.0f to 0.5f] of the visual size) or absolute (in world units).         It is absolute by default.         When the policy is relative, the corner radius is relative to the smaller of the visual's width and height.

properties.name: CornerSquareness
properties.description: The squareness for the rounded corners of the visual.         The values in Vector4 are used in clockwise order from top-left to bottom-left : Vector4(top-left-corner, top-right-corner, bottom-right-corner, bottom-left-corner).         Each radius will clamp internally between 0.0 and 1.0.

properties.name: BorderlineWidth
properties.description: The width for the borderline of the visual.

properties.name: BorderlineColor
properties.description: The color for the borderline of the visual.         It is Color.Black by default.

properties.name: BorderlineOffset
properties.description: The Relative offset for the borderline of the visual.         Recommended range : [-1.0f to 1.0f].         If -1.0f, draw borderline inside of the visual.         If 1.0f, draw borderline outside of the visual.         If 0.0f, draw borderline half inside and half outside.         It is 0.0f by default.

widgets.name: GradientVisual
widgets.description: Visual to render gradient.

properties.name: StartPosition
properties.description: Visual to render gradient.

properties.name: EndPosition
properties.description:
Gets or sets the end position of a linear gradient.
Mandatory for linear.

properties.name: Center
properties.description:
Gets or sets the center point of a radial gradient.
Mandatory for radial and conic.

properties.name: Radius
properties.description:
Gets or sets the size of the radius of a radial gradient.
Mandatory for radial.

properties.name: StopOffsets
properties.description:
Gets or sets all the stop offsets.
A PropertyArray of float.
If not supplied, the default is 0.0f and 1.0f.
Optional.

properties.name: StopColors
properties.description:
Gets or sets the color at the stop offsets.
A PropertyArray of color.
At least 2 values are required to show a gradient.
Mandatory.

properties.name: Units
properties.description:
Gets or sets descriptions of the coordinate system for certain attributes of the points in a gradient.
If not supplied, the default is GradientVisualUnitsType.ObjectBoundingBox.
Optional.

properties.name: SpreadMethod
properties.description:
Gets or sets indications of what happens if the gradient starts or ends inside the bounds of the target rectangle.
If not supplied, the default is GradientVisualSpreadMethodType.Pad.
Optional.

properties.name: StartOffset
properties.description:
Gets or sets the gradient's start position offset.
If not supplied, the default is 0.0f.
Optional.

properties.name: StartAngle
properties.description:
Gets or sets the start angle of the conic gradient.
Mandatory for conic.
Optional.

widgets.name: ImageVisual
widgets.description: The visual which can display an image resource.

properties.name: ResourceUrl
properties.description: The visual which can display an image resource.

properties.name: PixelArea
properties.description:
Gets or sets the image area to be displayed.
It is a rectangular area.
The first two elements indicate the top-left position of the area, and the last two elements are the areas of the width and the height respectively.
If not specified, the default value is Vector4 (0.0, 0.0, 1.0, 1.0), i.e., the entire area of the image.
For normal quad images only.
Optional.

properties.name: PreMultipliedAlpha
properties.description:
ImageView PreMultipliedAlpha, type Boolean.
Image must be initialized.

properties.name: SynchronousLoading
properties.description: Synchronously load the image for the visual.

properties.name: OrientationCorrection
properties.description: Gets or sets whether to automatically correct the orientation of an image.

properties.name: SynchronousSizing
properties.description:
Gets or sets whether to automatically reload the image as the visual size.
If we set this value as true, Visual size will be works as desired size of image.

properties.name: AlphaMaskUrl
properties.description:
Gets or sets the URL of the alpha mask.
Optional.

properties.name: MaskContentScale
properties.description: Gets or sets scale factor to apply to the content image before masking.

properties.name: CropToMask
properties.description: Whether to crop image to mask or scale mask to fit image.

properties.name: FastTrackUploading
properties.description: Gets or sets whether to apply mask on GPU or not.

properties.name: ReleasePolicy
properties.description:
Gets or sets the Image Visual release policy.
It decides if a texture should be released from the cache or kept to reduce the loading time.

properties.name: SamplingMode
properties.description:
Gets or sets filtering options used when resizing images to the sample original pixels.
If not supplied, the default is SamplingModeType.BoxThenLinear.
For normal quad images only.
Optional.

properties.name: DesiredWidth
properties.description:
Gets or sets the desired image width.
If not specified, the actual image width is used.
For normal quad images only.
Optional.

properties.name: DesiredHeight
properties.description:
Gets or sets the desired image height.
If not specified, the actual image height is used.
For normal quad images only.
Optional.

properties.name: LoadPolicy
properties.description:
Gets or sets the Image Visual image loading policy.
It decides if a texture should be loaded immediately after source set or only after the visual is added to the window.

properties.name: WrapModeU
properties.description:
Gets or sets the wrap mode for the u coordinate.
It decides how the texture should be sampled when the u coordinate exceeds the range of 0.0 to 1.0.
If not specified, the default is WrapModeType.Default(CLAMP).
For normal quad images only.
Optional.

properties.name: WrapModeV
properties.description:
Gets or sets the wrap mode for the v coordinate.
It decides how the texture should be sampled when the v coordinate exceeds the range of 0.0 to 1.0.
The first two elements indicate the top-left position of the area, and the last two elements are the areas of the width and the height respectively.
If not specified, the default is WrapModeType.Default(CLAMP).
For normal quad images only. Optional.

properties.name: CornerRadius
properties.description: The radius for the rounded corners of the visual.         The values in Vector4 are used in clockwise order from top-left to bottom-left : Vector4(top-left-corner, top-right-corner, bottom-right-corner, bottom-left-corner).         Each radius will clamp internally to the half of smaller of the visual's width or height.

properties.name: CornerRadiusPolicy
properties.description: Whether the CornerRadius property value is relative (percentage [0.0f to 0.5f] of the visual size) or absolute (in world units).         It is absolute by default.         When the policy is relative, the corner radius is relative to the smaller of the visual's width and height.

properties.name: CornerSquareness
properties.description: The squareness for the rounded corners of the visual.         The values in Vector4 are used in clockwise order from top-left to bottom-left : Vector4(top-left-corner, top-right-corner, bottom-right-corner, bottom-left-corner).         Each radius will clamp internally between [0.0f to 1.0f].

properties.name: BorderlineWidth
properties.description: The width for the borderline of the visual.

properties.name: BorderlineColor
properties.description: The color for the borderline of the visual.         It is Color.Black by default.

properties.name: BorderlineOffset
properties.description: The Relative offset for the borderline of the visual.         Recommended range : [-1.0f to 1.0f].         If -1.0f, draw borderline inside of the visual.         If 1.0f, draw borderline outside of the visual.         If 0.0f, draw borderline half inside and half outside.         It is 0.0f by default.

properties.name: Border
properties.description: The border of the regular image is in the order: left, right, bottom, top.

properties.name: AuxiliaryImageUrl
properties.description: Overlays the auxiliary image on top of an NPatch image.         The resulting visual image will be at least as large as the smallest possible n-patch or the auxiliary image, whichever is larger.

properties.name: AuxiliaryImageAlpha
properties.description: An alpha value for mixing between the masked main NPatch image and the auxiliary image.

The GLWindow class is to draw with native GLES.

So, some special functions and type are supported.
In addition, basic window's functions are supported, too.

properties.description: Sets position and size of the window. This API guarantees that         both moving and resizing of window will appear on the screen at once.

properties.name: PartialUpdate
properties.description: Gets or sets whether the window will update partial area or full area.         If this value is true, window will update and render partial area.         If false, full area updated.

properties.name: NativeHandle
properties.description: Gets the number of currently alived Window object.

properties.name: IsModal
properties.description: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the window is modal or not.         The modal property of a window requires that it be set to a parent window.         The window modal function operates on the specified parent window.

properties.name: IsAlwaysOnTop
properties.description: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the window is always on top of other windows or not.         This is valid between windows that have no notification level or a notification level of 'none'.         If it has a notification level, this will not do anything.

properties.name: IsBottom
properties.description: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the window is the bottom of other windows or not.         If the enable flag is true, this window will be placed below other windows.         Otherwise, if it's called with a false value, it will be located above other windows..

methods.name: GrabKeyTopmost
methods.description:
Grabs the key specified by a key for a window only when a window is the topmost window.
This function can be used for following example scenarios:
- Mobile - Using volume up or down as zoom up or down in camera apps.

methods.name: UngrabKeyTopmost
methods.description:
Ungrabs the key specified by a key for the window.
Note: If this function is called between key down and up events of a grabbed key, an application doesn't receive the key up event.

methods.name: GrabKey
methods.description:
Grabs the key specified by a key for a window in a GrabMode.
Details: This function can be used for following example scenarios:
- TV - A user might want to change the volume or channel of the background TV contents while focusing on the foregrund app.
- Mobile - When a user presses the Home key, the homescreen appears regardless of the current foreground app.
- Mobile - Using the volume up or down as zoom up or down in camera apps.

methods.name: UngrabKey
methods.description:
Ungrabs the key specified by a key for a window.
Note: If this function is called between key down and up events of a grabbed key, an application doesn't receive the key up event.

methods.name: SetParent
methods.description: Sets parent window of the window.         After setting that, these windows do together when raise-up, lower and iconified/deiconified.         Initially, the window is located on top of the parent. The window can go below parent by calling Lower().         If parent's window stack is changed by calling Raise() or Lower(), child windows are located on top of the parent again.

methods.name: SetParent
methods.description: Sets parent window of the window.         After setting that, these windows do together when raise-up, lower and iconified/deiconified.         This function has the additional flag whether the child is located above or below of the parent.

methods.description: Unsets parent window of the window.         After unsetting, the window is disconnected his parent window.

methods.name: IncludeInputRegion
methods.description: Includes input region.         This function inlcudes input regions.         It can be used multiple times and supports multiple regions.         It means input region will be extended.         This input is related to mouse and touch event.         If device has touch screen, this function is useful.         Otherwise device does not have that, we can use it after connecting mouse to the device.

methods.name: ExcludeInputRegion
methods.description: This function excludes input regions.         It can be used multiple times and supports multiple regions.         It means input region will be reduced.         Nofice, should be set input area by IncludeInputRegion() before this function is used.         This input is related to mouse and touch event.         If device has touch screen, this function is useful.         Otherwise device does not have that, we can use it after connecting mouse to the device.

methods.description: Maximizes window's size.         If this function is called with true, window will be resized with screen size.         Otherwise window will be resized with previous size.         It is for the window's MAX button in window's border.         If window border is supported by display server, it is not necessary.

methods.name: Maximize
methods.description: Sets the maximized state of a given window with specific size         If this function is called with true, window will be resized with screen size.         Otherwise window will be resized with previous size.         It is for the window's MAX button in window's border.         If window border is supported by display server, it is not necessary.

methods.name: IsMaximized
methods.description: Returns whether the window is maximized or not.

methods.name: SetMaximumSize
methods.description: Sets window's maximum size.         It is to set the maximized size when window is maximized or the window's size is increased by RequestResizeToServer().         Although the size is set by this function, window's size can be increased over the limitation by SetPositionSize() or SetSize().         After setting, if Maximize() is called, window is resized with the setting size and move the center.

methods.name: Minimize
methods.description: Minimizes window's size.         If this function is called with true, window will be iconified.         Otherwise window will be activated.         It is for the window's MIN button in window border.         If window border is supported by display server, it is not necessary.

methods.name: IsMinimized
methods.description: Returns whether the window is minimized or not.

methods.name: SetMimimumSize
methods.description: Sets window's minimum size.         It is to set the minimum size when window's size is decreased by RequestResizeToServer().         Although the size is set by this function, window's size can be decreased over the limitation by SetPositionSize() or SetSize().

methods.description: Get Native Window handle. How to get Native Window handle Window window = NUIApplication.GetDefaultWindow(); var handle = window.NativeHandle; if(handle.IsInvalid == false) { IntPtr nativeHandle = handle.DangerousGetHandle(); // do something with nativeHandle }

methods.name: UpdateLayout
methods.description: Sets or gets the window blur using window blur information.         It is designed to apply a blur effect to a window based on specified parameters.         This supports different types of blurring effects, including blurring the window's background only.         Or blurring the area surrounding the window while keeping the window itself clear.         The more information is written WindowBlurInfo struct.

widgets.name: Window
widgets.description:
The window class is used internally for drawing.
The window has an orientation and indicator properties.

properties.name: Window
properties.description:
The window class is used internally for drawing.
The window has an orientation and indicator properties.

properties.name: FocusChanged
properties.description: This event is triggered when the focus changes on the window.

properties.name: TouchEvent
properties.description:
Emits the event when the screen is touched and when the touch ends.
If there are multiple touch points then it is emitted when the first touch occurs and when the last finger is lifted too.
Even though incoming events are interrupted, the event occurs.

properties.name: InterceptTouchEvent
properties.description:
An event for the touched signal which can be used to subscribe or unsubscribe the event handler provided by the user.
The touched signal is emitted when the touch input is received.
This can receive touch events before child.
If it returns false, the child can receive the touch event. If it returns true, the touch event is intercepted. So child cannot receive touch event.

properties.name: WheelEvent
properties.description: Emits the event when the wheel event is received.

properties.name: InterceptWheelEvent
properties.description:
An event for the wheel event signal which can be used to subscribe or unsubscribe the event handler provided by the user.
The wheel event signal is emitted when the wheel input is received.
This can receive wheel events before child.
If it returns false, the child can receive the wheel event. If it returns true, the wheel event is intercepted. So child cannot receive wheel event.

properties.name: KeyEvent
properties.description: Emits the event when the key event is received.

properties.name: InterceptKeyEvent
properties.description:
Intercepts KeyEvents in the window before dispatching KeyEvents to the child.
If it returns true(consumed), no KeyEvent is delivered to the child.

properties.name: Resized
properties.description: Emits the event when window is resized by user or the display server.

properties.name: HoverEvent
properties.description:
An event for the hovered signal which can be used to subscribe or unsubscribe the event handler provided by the user.
The hovered signal is emitted when the hover input is received.

properties.name: WindowFocusChanged
properties.description: Do not use this, that will be deprecated. Use 'FocusChanged' event instead.

properties.name: TransitionEffect
properties.description: EffectStart

properties.name: Moved
properties.description: Emits the event when window is moved by user or the display server.

properties.name: OrientationChanged
properties.description: Window Orientation Changed event         This event is for per windows

properties.name: KeyboardRepeatSettingsChanged
properties.description: Keyboard Repeat Settings Changed

properties.name: MouseInOutEvent
properties.description: MouseInOutEvent event.

properties.name: MoveCompleted
properties.description:
Emits the event when window has been moved by the display server.
To make the window move by display server, RequestMoveToServer() should be called.
After the moving job is completed, this signal will be emitted.

properties.name: ResizeCompleted
properties.description:
Emits the event when window has been resized by the display server.
To make the window resize by display server, RequestResizeToServer() should be called.
After the resizing job is completed, this signal will be emitted.

properties.name: MouseRelativeEvent
properties.description: Emits the event when relative mouse movement occurs in the window.

properties.name: PointerConstraintsEvent
properties.description: Emits the event when pointer is locked/unlocked

properties.name: DispatchTouchMotion
properties.description: Gets or sets the status of whether motion event of Touch can be dispatched.         If a Window's DispatchTouchMotion is set to false, then it's can not will receive motion event of TouchEvent.

properties.name: DispatchHoverMotion
properties.description: Gets or sets the status of whether motion event of Hover can be dispatched.         If a Window's DispatchHoverMotion is set to false, then it's can not will receive motion event of HoverEvent.

properties.name: FocusGained
properties.description:
System-level key event interception that occurs before the user-level InterceptKeyEvent.
This event is intended for internal framework use and provides the highest priority for intercepting key events.
When this event returns true (consumed), it prevents the key event from being dispatched to both InterceptKeyEvent and child views.
Multiple handlers can be registered, and their return values are OR'd together.
This event is marked as internal and should not be used by application code.
